gradeEntryComponentId.description: CurriculumPlan componentId for the GradeEntry feed (grading spec, forward-ref).
gradeEntryComponentId.title: Grade Entry Component ID

competencyIds.description: UUIDs of Competency taxonomy nodes (competency capability) a graded AssessmentResult for this assessment provides evidence for, following the same shape as Assignment.competencyIds. Additive field — existing rows leave it empty.
competencyIds.title: Competency IDs

releaseConditions.description: Per-learner adaptive-release conditions, AND-combined, mirroring Lesson.releaseConditions exactly. Layered ON TOP of the existing availableFrom/availableUntil absolute window (unchanged) — an Assessment is available to a given learner only when both the absolute window and every listed releaseConditions entry are satisfied. Evaluated per-learner at request time by the shared LessonReleaseEvaluator — NOT materialised as a schema-level calculation (same reasoning as Lesson.releaseConditions).

kind.description: Condition kind. lesson-completed requires an XapiStatement completion/passed verb for lessonId; assessment-min-score requires a graded AssessmentResult for assessmentId summing to at least minScore.
kind.title: Condition Kind

lessonId.description: UUID of the prerequisite Lesson. Required when kind is lesson-completed.
lessonId.title: Prerequisite Lesson ID

assessmentId.description: UUID of the prerequisite Assessment. Required when kind is assessment-min-score.
assessmentId.title: Prerequisite Assessment ID

minScore.description: Minimum summed score required on the referenced Assessment. Required when kind is assessment-min-score.
minScore.title: Minimum Score

releaseConditions.title: Release Conditions

availableAfterDays.description: Drip-release delay: number of days after the learner's OWN Enrolment.created timestamp (for this Assessment's courseId) before the assessment becomes available to that learner, mirroring Lesson.availableAfterDays exactly. Null = no drip delay. Applies in addition to both the existing absolute availableFrom/availableUntil window and any releaseConditions — an Assessment is available to a learner only once all three are satisfied.
availableAfterDays.title: Available After Days (Drip)

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle state. Managed by OR's lifecycle engine.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

isProctored.description: True when proctoring configuration is non-null.

isAvailable.description: True when now is within the availableFrom/availableUntil window.

AssessmentResult.title: AssessmentResult
AssessmentResult.description: A learner's attempt at an Assessment. Append-only — a finished attempt is immutable evidence. Auto-scored items receive autoScore on the submit transition; essay items remain null until a teacher provides manualScore.

assessmentId.description: UUID of the Assessment being attempted.
assessmentId.title: Assessment ID

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner taking the assessment.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

attemptNumber.description: Attempt sequence number for this learner on this assessment.
attemptNumber.title: Attempt Number

itemId.title: Item ID
itemId.description: UUID of the item this response is for.

response.description: Learner's response data (type depends on interactionType).
response.title: Response Data

autoScore.description: Score assigned by auto-scoring. Null if not yet scored or needsManualScoring.
autoScore.title: Auto Score

manualScore.description: Score assigned by a teacher. Required for extendedText items before grading.
manualScore.title: Manual Score

responses.description: Per-item responses with auto and manual scores.
responses.title: Item Responses

itemId.title: Item ID
itemId.description: UUID of an item presented in this attempt.

points.title: Points
points.description: Points this item was worth in this attempt (itemRefs override for fixed; drawn Item.maxScore for random-draw).

optionOrder.title: Option Order
optionOrder.description: QTI simpleChoice identifier values in presentation order, when shuffleAnswerOptions is true and the item exposes discrete choice identifiers. Null for essay/textEntry items or when shuffle is disabled.

drawnItemRefs.description: Frozen, server-resolved snapshot of exactly which items (and, when shuffled, which order and answer-option order) this attempt presented. Populated once, at creation, by AssessmentDrawResolver (an ObjectCreatedEvent listener) — NEVER client-set and NEVER recomputed afterward, so a review/appeal can reconstruct exactly what the learner saw regardless of later edits to the Assessment, ItemBank, or Item objects. Array position IS presentation order. Populated for every attempt, fixed or random-draw.
drawnItemRefs.title: Drawn Item References

startedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the attempt was started.
startedAt.title: Started At

submittedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the attempt was submitted. Null while in-progress.
submittedAt.title: Submitted At

proctoringSessionId.description: UUID of the associated ProctoringSession. Null for unproctored attempts.
proctoringSessionId.title: Proctoring Session ID

gradeEntryId.description: UUID of the GradeEntry created for this result (grading spec, forward-ref).
gradeEntryId.title: Grade Entry ID

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle state. Managed by OR's lifecycle engine.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

x-property-rbac._comment: WF1 (wave-12): assessment results contain responses and scores — student PII. Learner sees own results; admins see all.

ItemStatistics.title: ItemStatistics
ItemStatistics.description: Classical-test-theory statistics for one Item within one Assessment (p-value, corrected item-total correlation, distractor analysis), computed by ItemAnalysisService from graded AssessmentResults and upserted by ItemAnalysisRecomputeHandler whenever a result for the assessment reaches graded. No lifecycle — fully derived, read-only. Do not set manually. Scoped per (itemId, assessmentId): the same item can behave differently across cohorts or when reused in a different assessment.

itemId.description: UUID of the Item these statistics describe.
itemId.title: Item ID

assessmentId.description: UUID of the Assessment this item was administered in.
assessmentId.title: Assessment ID

sampleSize.description: Number of graded AssessmentResults referencing this item for this assessment.
sampleSize.title: Sample Size

pValue.description: Proportion of respondents who scored full marks on this item. Null when sampleSize is below the configured minSampleSize (default 20) — insufficientData is true in that case.
pValue.title: p-value (Difficulty)

itemTotalCorrelation.description: Corrected item-total (item-rest) Pearson correlation: the item's per-respondent score vector against each respondent's total score computed EXCLUDING this item's own contribution. Null when sampleSize is below the configured minSampleSize.
itemTotalCorrelation.title: Item-Total Correlation (Discrimination)

selectedByHighGroup.title: Selected By High Group
selectedByHighGroup.description: Number of top-27%-by-total-score respondents who selected this option.

selectedByLowGroup.title: Selected By Low Group
selectedByLowGroup.description: Number of bottom-27%-by-total-score respondents who selected this option.

distractorAnalysis.description: Per-option selection counts split by the Kelley top/bottom 27% scoring groups, for choice-type items with a declared correctResponse. Null for non-choice items or when sampleSize is below the configured minSampleSize.
distractorAnalysis.title: Distractor Analysis

insufficientData.description: True when sampleSize is below the configured minSampleSize — pValue/itemTotalCorrelation/distractorAnalysis are null and MUST NOT be presented as reportable numbers.
insufficientData.title: Insufficient Data

computedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last recompute.
computedAt.title: Computed At

x-property-rbac._comment: assessment-item-pools-and-analysis: item difficulty/discrimination statistics MUST NOT be visible to learners — that data would let a learner infer which items are safe to guess on, undermining validity for everyone else. Staff-only, mirroring CompetencyAttainment's admin/teacher role-wide shape plus examboard (the WHW art. 7.13 accountable body).

AssessmentReliability.title: AssessmentReliability
AssessmentReliability.description: Cronbach's alpha internal-consistency reliability for one Assessment as a whole, computed by ItemAnalysisService from graded AssessmentResults and upserted by ItemAnalysisRecomputeHandler whenever a result for the assessment reaches graded. No lifecycle — fully derived, read-only. Do not set manually.

assessmentId.description: UUID of the Assessment this reliability figure describes.
assessmentId.title: Assessment ID

sampleSize.description: Number of graded AssessmentResults for this assessment.
sampleSize.title: Sample Size

itemCount.description: Number of distinct items scored across those results (k in the alpha formula).
itemCount.title: Item Count

cronbachAlpha.description: Internal-consistency reliability: alpha = (k/(k-1)) * (1 - sum(item variances) / total-score variance). Null when sampleSize is below the configured reliabilityMinSampleSize (default 30) or itemCount < 2 — insufficientData is true in that case.
cronbachAlpha.title: Cronbach's Alpha

insufficientData.description: True when sampleSize is below reliabilityMinSampleSize or itemCount < 2 — cronbachAlpha is null and MUST NOT be presented as a reportable number.
insufficientData.title: Insufficient Data

computedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last recompute.
computedAt.title: Computed At

x-property-rbac._comment: assessment-item-pools-and-analysis: same staff-only posture as ItemStatistics — an assessment's reliability figure MUST NOT be visible to learners.

ItemRevisionFlag.title: ItemRevisionFlag
ItemRevisionFlag.description: Created by ItemAnalysisRecomputeHandler when an ItemStatistics computation (with insufficientData false) crosses a configured quality threshold (too-difficult, too-easy, low-discrimination, negative-discrimination). appendOnly: true per ADR-008 (immutable audit evidence), mirroring AttendanceFlag/BsaProgressFlag/EngagementRiskFlag. A review signal ONLY — creation MUST NOT alter the flagged Item automatically. Routed to the examboard/admin groups, the same groups FraudCase notifications already use (WHW art. 7.13 toetscommissie/examencommissie authority).

subject.nl: Een item is gemarkeerd voor herziening
subject.en: An item has been flagged for revision

itemStatisticsId.description: UUID of the ItemStatistics computation that triggered this flag.
itemStatisticsId.title: Item Statistics ID

pValueAtFlag.description: Snapshot of ItemStatistics.pValue at the moment this flag was raised.
pValueAtFlag.title: p-value At Flag

itemTotalCorrelationAtFlag.description: Snapshot of ItemStatistics.itemTotalCorrelation at the moment this flag was raised.
itemTotalCorrelationAtFlag.title: Item-Total Correlation At Flag

flaggedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this flag was raised.
flaggedAt.title: Flagged At

lifecycle.description: Flag lifecycle state. Managed by OR lifecycle engine.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

x-property-rbac._comment: assessment-item-pools-and-analysis: same staff-only posture as ItemStatistics/AssessmentReliability.

ProctoringSession.title: ProctoringSession
ProctoringSession.description: Created when a proctored Assessment is started. Append-only — a completed session is immutable evidence. Flags raised by the provider are reviewed by an invigilator; a flag NEVER auto-alters the associated AssessmentResult (EU AI Act Art. 14 human oversight).

assessmentResultId.description: UUID of the AssessmentResult this session covers.
assessmentResultId.title: Assessment Result ID

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner being proctored.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

provider.description: Proctoring adapter identifier; resolves to ProvidesProctoring implementation.
provider.title: Proctoring Provider

providerSessionId.description: Provider-side session identifier. Null until the provider confirms creation.
providerSessionId.title: Provider Session ID

recordedArtefactRefs.description: URLs or OR attachment refs for recorded artefacts. Actual bytes stay with the provider.
recordedArtefactRefs.title: Recorded Artefact References

kind.description: Flag category (e.g. gaze-away, audio-event, object-detected).
kind.title: Flag Kind

occurredAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the event occurred.
occurredAt.title: Occurred At

reviewDecision.description: Invigilator decision. Changed ONLY by a human invigilator — never by automated logic (EU AI Act Art. 14).
reviewDecision.title: Review Decision

reviewedBy.description: Nextcloud user ID of the invigilator who reviewed this flag.
reviewedBy.title: Reviewed By

reviewedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the review decision.
reviewedAt.title: Reviewed At

flags.description: Flagged proctoring events. Each flag requires invigilator review before any consequence is applied to the learner.
flags.title: Proctoring Flags

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle state. Managed by OR's lifecycle engine.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

x-openregister-authorization._comment: secure-exam-test-mode: first change to ever write this schema — a learner's own browser (native test mode) or an external provider adapter creates the session, so create must be reachable by an authenticated user, not admin-only (contrast XapiStatement, which is admin-only pending a dedicated ingest controller). Object-level scoping (only the named learnerId) and field-level write RBAC on flags[].reviewDecision are NOT enforced here — known platform gap, tracked in design.md §4.2/§6.

GradeScale.title: GradeScale
GradeScale.description: A named grading scale defining the value range, bands, rounding rule, and pass threshold for marks. Supports numeric (NL 1.0–10.0), letter (A–F), ECTS (A–F), pass/fail, percentage, and custom band sets.

kind.description: Discriminator for how values and bands are interpreted.
kind.title: Scale Kind

bands.description: Ordered grade bands (for letter/ects/band kinds). Each band covers a value range and carries a pass flag.

bandId.description: Stable identifier for this band, e.g. 'A', 'B', 'voldoende'.
bandId.title: Band ID

minValue.description: Inclusive lower bound of the raw value range for this band.
minValue.title: Minimum Value

maxValue.description: Inclusive upper bound of the raw value range for this band.
maxValue.title: Maximum Value

pass.description: Whether this band is considered passing.
pass.title: Passing Band

bands.title: Grade Bands

min.description: Minimum possible value (for numeric/percentage kinds). Null for band-based scales.
min.title: Minimum Grade Value

max.description: Maximum possible value (for numeric/percentage kinds). Null for band-based scales.
max.title: Maximum Grade Value

passThreshold.description: Minimum value required to pass (for numeric/percentage kinds). Values below this threshold yield passed=false.
passThreshold.title: Pass Threshold

roundingRule.description: Rounding rule applied before comparing to passThreshold or displaying the grade.
roundingRule.title: Rounding Rule

lifecycle.description: Scale lifecycle state. Managed by OR lifecycle engine.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

GradeEntry.title: GradeEntry
GradeEntry.description: One mark on one CurriculumPlan component for one learner. Starts as concept (editable); a teacher publishes it to notify the learner (and parents) and feed the FinalGrade roll-up. Once published, it can only move to revised (new value with audit trail). Not appendOnly — concept entries are editable; lifecycle enforces immutability once published.

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner this mark belongs to.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

learnerRef.description: UUID reference to the LearnerProfile object — the portal-subject scoping key (ADR-046 A4). Distinct from learnerId, which is the Nextcloud user id. Additive and optional; existing rows leave it unset and stay invisible to the portal (fail-closed) until backfilled.
learnerRef.title: Learner Ref

curriculumPlanId.description: UUID of the CurriculumPlan this entry contributes to.
curriculumPlanId.title: Curriculum Plan ID

courseId.description: UUID of the associated Course. Null when scope is set via cohort only.
courseId.title: Course ID

submissionId.description: UUID of the Submission that originated this mark (set when sourceKind=assignment-submission).
submissionId.title: Submission ID

assessmentResultId.description: UUID of the AssessmentResult that originated this mark (set when sourceKind=assessment-result).
assessmentResultId.title: Assessment Result ID

sessionId.description: UUID of the Session that originated this mark (set when sourceKind=participation).
sessionId.title: Session ID

exemptionCaseId.description: UUID of the ExemptionCase that originated this mark (set when sourceKind=exemption, by ExemptionGrantHandler).
exemptionCaseId.title: Exemption Case ID

fraudCaseId.description: UUID of a FraudCase contesting this mark, if any. When set, FraudCaseBlockGuard gates the publish/republish transitions and FraudCaseInvalidationGuard gates the invalidate transition.
fraudCaseId.title: Fraud Case ID

ltiToolPlacementId.description: UUID of the LtiToolPlacement that originated this mark (set when sourceKind=lti-ags).
ltiToolPlacementId.title: LTI Tool Placement ID

ltiAgsResultId.description: The AGS resultId / CloudEvent message identifier for the score that originated this mark (set when sourceKind=lti-ags). Used as the idempotency key so a redelivered pull message cannot create a duplicate GradeEntry.
ltiAgsResultId.title: LTI AGS Result ID

portfolioId.description: UUID of the Portfolio that originated this mark (set when sourceKind=portfolio, by PortfolioGradeEmitHandler).
portfolioId.title: Portfolio ID

value.description: Raw mark value on the component's GradeScale (e.g. 7.5 for NL numeric). Required for every sourceKind except exemption, where it MUST be null — an exemption has no numeric mark, the exam board's decision is the pass signal (enforced by the allOf if/then below).
value.title: Grade Value

gradeScaleId.description: UUID of the GradeScale used to interpret and display this value.
gradeScaleId.title: Grade Scale ID

weight.description: Per-entry weight override. Null means fall back to the matching CurriculumPlan.components[].weight.
weight.title: Weight Override

grader.description: Nextcloud user ID of the teacher who entered this mark.
grader.title: Grader

gradedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the grade was entered.
gradedAt.title: Graded At

comment.description: Optional free-text feedback or annotation from the grader.
comment.title: Grader Comment

visibleFrom.description: Earliest datetime at which this entry's gradePublished notification is eligible to fire. May be set explicitly by the teacher as a publish-time override; when left unset it is resolved server-side by GradeVisibilityResolver from the governing CurriculumPlan.gradeVisibilityPolicy at the moment the entry transitions to published. Null before first publish.
visibleFrom.title: Visible From

lifecycle.description: Entry lifecycle state. Concept = editable, published = immutable + notified, revised = superseded by a new published entry, invalidated = terminal state reached only from concept when a linked FraudCase is decided fraud-proven — never published.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

allOf._comment: exam-board-case-handling: conditional value requiredness by sourceKind. Standard JSON Schema if/then/else — no prior precedent for this construct in this register (documented in design.md §7); value is required and numeric for every sourceKind except exemption, where it MUST be null.

x-openregister-lifecycle._comment: report-card: publish/republish's requires guard is OCA\Scholiq\Lifecycle\ReportPeriodLockGuard, which composes the original OCA\Scholiq\Lifecycle\FraudCaseBlockGuard check internally (constructor-injected, called first, unchanged behaviour) and then additionally blocks publishing into a locked report-card ReportPeriod's scope unless the actor holds admin/mentor/principal. requires is a single DI-tag string in this register's lifecycle engine (verified: OpenRegister's LifecycleAnnotationValidator rejects a non-string requires value) — there is no 'second requires entry' array shape to add alongside FraudCaseBlockGuard, so the guard composes rather than stacks.

x-property-rbac._comment: WF1 (wave-12): grade entries are sensitive academic PII. Learner sees own grades; admins see all. exam-board-case-handling: examboard role added so a board member can read the specific grade their ExemptionCase or FraudCase concerns.

FinalGrade.title: FinalGrade
FinalGrade.description: Calculated roll-up per learner per Course or Programme. Recomputed whenever a GradeEntry for this (learnerId, curriculumPlanId) publishes — applying the CurriculumPlan.formula over the learner's published GradeEntries. No lifecycle — it is fully derived. Read-only from the frontend.

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner this grade belongs to.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

learnerRef.description: UUID reference to the LearnerProfile object — the portal-subject scoping key (ADR-046 A4). Distinct from learnerId, which is the Nextcloud user id. Additive and optional; existing rows leave it unset and stay invisible to the portal (fail-closed) until backfilled.
learnerRef.title: Learner Ref

courseId.description: UUID of the Course this final grade applies to. Null for programme-level grades.
courseId.title: Course ID

programmeId.description: UUID of the Programme this final grade applies to. Null for course-level grades.
programmeId.title: Programme ID

curriculumPlanId.description: UUID of the CurriculumPlan whose formula is applied.
curriculumPlanId.title: Curriculum Plan ID

gradeScaleId.description: UUID of the GradeScale used to interpret the computed value.
gradeScaleId.title: Grade Scale ID

value.description: Computed final grade value. Null before any GradeEntry has published. Derived — do not set manually.
value.title: Final Grade Value

passed.description: Whether the learner has passed, per the formula + passRules + GradeScale.passThreshold. Null before enough entries have published.
passed.title: Passed

breakdown.description: Per-period averages and per-component contributions as a JSON object. Derived — do not set manually. exam-board-case-handling: a component satisfied by a sourceKind=exemption GradeEntry instead carries components[componentId] = { exempt: true } (no value/weight/contribution — see GradeFormulaEvaluator::weightedAverage()).
breakdown.title: Grade Breakdown

lastRecomputedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last recompute triggered by a GradeEntry publish.
lastRecomputedAt.title: Last Recomputed At

publishedEntries.description: Cross-schema aggregation: number of published GradeEntries for this learner + curriculumPlan. The full entry list consumed by the formula evaluation is queried by GradeRollupHandler in PHP.

calculatedChange.description: Recompute this FinalGrade whenever a GradeEntry for (learnerId, curriculumPlanId) publishes. Implemented via GradeRollupHandler listening on ObjectTransitionedEvent — NOT a TimedJob.

x-property-rbac._comment: WF1 (wave-12): final grades are academic PII. Learner sees own final grade; admins see all. exam-board-case-handling: examboard role added, same reasoning as GradeEntry.

ReportPeriod.title: ReportPeriod
ReportPeriod.description: The school-wide term a periodic report card (rapport) is composed for: name, academic year, a periodCode matching the same period identifier GradeEntry.period / CurriculumPlan.components[].period / FinalGrade.breakdown.periods keys already use, the window AttendanceRecord is summarised over, which CurriculumPlans (subjects) and Cohorts it covers, and a nullable lockDate after which ordinary grade publishing for that period is blocked (report-card spec).

academicYear.description: Academic year in YYYY-YYYY format, same convention as Cohort.academicYear/BsaTrajectory.academicYear.
academicYear.title: Academic Year

periodCode.description: Period identifier MUST match GradeEntry.period, CurriculumPlan.components[].period, and the key used in FinalGrade.breakdown.periods — this is the join key the composer uses, not a new period-numbering scheme.
periodCode.title: Period Code

startDate.description: Start date of the window AttendanceRecord (via its Session.startsAt) is summarised over.
startDate.title: Start Date

endDate.description: End date of the window AttendanceRecord (via its Session.startsAt) is summarised over.
endDate.title: End Date

curriculumPlanIds.description: UUIDs of the CurriculumPlans (subjects) that count toward this report.
curriculumPlanIds.title: Curriculum Plan IDs

cohortIds.description: UUIDs of the Cohorts this period's report cards cover, same shape as ConferenceRound.cohortIds[].
cohortIds.title: Cohort IDs

lockDate.description: Datetime after which ordinary teacher grade-publishing for this period is blocked (see isLocked below and grading's ReportPeriodLockGuard). Null = never locked.
lockDate.title: Lock Date

attendanceIncluded.description: Whether ReportCardComposer composes an attendanceSummary section for report cards in this period.
attendanceIncluded.title: Attendance Included

lifecycle.description: Period lifecycle state. open = still accepting grade changes / not yet composed; composed = ReportCards have been generated for every learner in scope; archived = retained, no longer active. Managed by the OR lifecycle engine.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

isLocked.description: True once lockDate is set AND has passed @now. Same lt/now idiom as ConferenceRound.isBookingClosed — informational/read directly by guards, NOT itself a lifecycle transition: a scheduled x-openregister-notifications trigger can only fire a notification, never drive a lifecycle transition (verified precedent: ConferenceRound.isBookingClosed's own description). ReportPeriodComposeGuard (compose) and grading's ReportPeriodLockGuard (GradeEntry.publish/republish) read this field directly via ObjectService.

subject.nl: De sluitingsdatum voor een rapportperiode is verstreken — start de samenstelling handmatig
subject.en: A report period's lock date has passed — start composition manually

x-openregister-authorization._comment: Tightest-posture pattern: no dedicated coordinator role exists in LearnerProfile.roles today (documented platform gap, same rationale as SupportRequest/TlvApplication/PupilVoice). Where a notification needs to reach 'the coordinator' this schema uses the already-precedented x-openregister-notifications kind:'groups' NC-group recipient shape above, not a fabricated RBAC role.

x-property-rbac._comment: ReportPeriod is the school's scheduling config, not a learner/parent-readable document — staff-only, mirrors ReportCard's staff half.

ReportCard.title: ReportCard
ReportCard.description: One composed periodic report (rapport) per (learner, ReportPeriod): a snapshot of each in-scope subject's period average and pass/fail state (from FinalGrade.breakdown.periods[periodCode]), a per-subject teacher comment, an attendance summary, an overall mentor comment, and a forward-compatible competencyAttainment section. Composed by ReportCardComposer on ReportPeriod.compose; reviewed at the rapportvergadering; published to parents only once every contributing grade's own visibleFrom has passed.

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner this report card belongs to.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

docudeskRenderError.title: Docudesk Render Error

lifecycle.description: Report card lifecycle state. draft = just composed, not yet under review; rapportvergadering-review = teacher/mentor comments being edited ahead of the deliberation meeting; finalised = locked pending publication; published-to-parents = visible in the parent portal. Managed by the OR lifecycle engine.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

recompose._comment: Self-loop, same shape already proven live in this register by ConferenceRound.regenerate (scheduled -> scheduled, no requires) and Credential.offerToWallet/recordWalletClaim (issued -> issued, requires a Service class) — from==to self-loops are a verified, working OR lifecycle-engine pattern here, not an open question. No requires guard: ReportCardComposer is a Listener (ADR-031 event-driven bridge), not a check()-shaped guard/service — it reacts to this transition's ObjectTransitionedEvent (schema=report-card, action=recompose) the same way it reacts to ReportPeriod's compose transition, both registered in lib/AppInfo/Application.php.

x-openregister-authorization._comment: Composer-created only in practice (ReportCardComposer via ObjectService, not the REST create endpoint); declarative create restriction still required per the tightest-posture pattern (no fabricated coordinator role — see ReportPeriod's identical rationale).

x-property-rbac._comment: Staff (admin/mentor/principal) see all; the learner sees their own report card, mirroring GradeEntry/FinalGrade's existing self-match shape exactly (no lifecycle gate at the RBAC layer, same posture those two schemas already have — the 'never before publication' promise is enforced by ReportCardVisibilityGuard at publishToParents and by the portal's parentReportCards collection filtering to published-to-parents only for guardians, not by this read RBAC). Guardian read is via the portal only, never a direct RBAC grant.

ReportCardParentNotification.title: ReportCardParentNotification
ReportCardParentNotification.description: Append-only parent-notification record created by ReportCardPublishHandler when a ReportCard transitions to published-to-parents. Structurally identical to GradeNotification's shape and reason — OR's declarative notification system addresses a single field (learnerId), not LearnerProfile.parentIds[], so a PHP fan-out bridge is required (mirrors GradeRollupHandler's parent fan-out half exactly). A separate schema rather than widening GradeNotification.event's enum, because GradeNotification.sourceId is typed $ref: GradeEntry — reusing it for a ReportCard source would make that reference lie about what it points to.

learnerRef.title: Learner Ref

visibleFrom.description: Earliest datetime at which this parent notification is eligible to fire. Stamped by ReportCardPublishHandler at creation (now()), same scheduled/visibleFrom/olderThan PT0S dispatch mechanism as GradeNotification.
visibleFrom.title: Visible From

Competency rows recurse under a CompetencyFramework via frameworkId. Lifecycle: draft → published → archived.

description.title: Framework Description

order.description: Ordinal position of this level in the scale, lowest to highest.
order.title: Order

proficiencyLevels.title: Proficiency Levels

A leaf Competency (isLeaf: true, childCount == 0) IS the learning outcome — no separate LearningOutcome schema exists. Lifecycle: draft → published → archived.

parentId.description: UUID of the parent Competency. Makes Competency recursive — mirrors Course.parentCourseId. Null for top-level nodes (e.g. a kerntaak or leerlijn).

title.title: Title

description.title: Description

order.description: Display order among sibling Competency nodes under the same parentId.
order.title: Order

requiredForRoles.description: LearnerProfile.roles values for which this competency is required — the 'required by role' half of the competency capability's skills-gap view.

lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

CompetencyAttainment.title: CompetencyAttainment
CompetencyAttainment.description: Calculated roll-up per learner per Competency. Recomputed whenever a competency-aligned GradeEntry (via Assignment/Assessment.competencyIds, resolved through Submission.assignmentId / AssessmentResult.assessmentId) publishes, or a WerkprocesAssessment with a resolved competencyId is confirmed. No lifecycle — it is fully derived. Read-only from the frontend. Mirrors FinalGrade's exact shape.

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner this attainment row belongs to.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

learnerRef.description: UUID reference to the LearnerProfile object — the portal-subject scoping key (ADR-046 A4). Distinct from learnerId, which is the Nextcloud user id. Additive and optional; existing rows leave it unset and stay invisible to the portal (fail-closed) until backfilled.
learnerRef.title: Learner Ref

competencyId.description: UUID of the Competency this row tracks attainment for.
competencyId.title: Competency ID

frameworkId.title: Framework ID

x-property-rbac._comment: wave-2 competency-framework: attainment rows are academic PII. Learner sees own rows; admin/hr/manager see all, grounded in dashboard/spec.md's named HR/manager audience.

x-openregister-processing.doelbinding: Record and adjudicate a learner's request to be exempted from a CurriculumPlan component on the strength of prior evidence (diploma, certificate, work experience), per WHW art. 7.13's mandate that the examencommissie make individual, reasoned exemption decisions consistent with the school's exemption policy (handreiking).

ExemptionCase.title: ExemptionCase
ExemptionCase.description: A learner's exemption (vrijstelling) request against one CurriculumPlan.components[] entry, decided by the exam board (examencommissie) on the strength of prior evidence (diploma, certificate, work experience) attached as OpenRegister file evidence. grant/reject require a recorded decisionRationale and policyReference (ExemptionDecisionGuard). On grant, ExemptionGrantHandler creates a GradeEntry (sourceKind: exemption, value: null) and drives it through the existing publish transition.

x-openregister-processing.doelbinding: Record and adjudicate a suspected academic-fraud or plagiarism allegation through report, hearing, reasoned decision, and (when proven) a capped sanction, per WHW art. 7.13 and the examencommissie's exclusive sanctioning authority — while the contested work stays blocked from publication for the duration of the case.
x-openregister-processing.retentionReference: not declared (fraud dossiers are examencommissie compliance + due-process evidence; align with the school's selectielijst onderwijs)

FraudCase.title: FraudCase
FraudCase.description: A fraud/plagiarism allegation against a learner's work: report → hearing record(s) → reasoned decision → sanction. Linked to the contested work via the same sourceKind/submissionId/assessmentResultId/sessionId shape GradeEntry already uses (a case can be filed before a GradeEntry exists), plus an optional contestedGradeEntryId once one does. decide requires a verdict + rationale (FraudCaseDecisionGuard); a fraud-proven verdict additionally requires a capped sanction and stamps a 42-day appealDeadline. FraudCaseBlockGuard blocks the linked GradeEntry's publish/republish while the case is open or permanently once fraud-proven; FraudCaseDecisionHandler invalidates a still-concept linked GradeEntry on a fraud-proven decision.

reporterId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the person (typically a teacher) who filed the case.
reporterId.title: Reporter ID

accusedLearnerId.description: UUID of the LearnerProfile the allegation is made against.
accusedLearnerId.title: Accused Learner ID

sourceKind.description: Origin of the contested work — same enum as GradeEntry.sourceKind, so a case can be filed before a GradeEntry exists.
sourceKind.title: Source Kind

submissionId.description: UUID of the contested Submission (set when sourceKind=assignment-submission).
submissionId.title: Submission ID

assessmentResultId.description: UUID of the contested AssessmentResult (set when sourceKind=assessment-result).
assessmentResultId.title: Assessment Result ID

sessionId.description: UUID of the contested Session (set when sourceKind=participation).
sessionId.title: Session ID

contestedGradeEntryId.description: UUID of the GradeEntry contesting this work, once one exists. Null when the case is filed before a GradeEntry exists.
contestedGradeEntryId.title: Contested Grade Entry ID

allegation.description: Free-text description of the suspected fraud/plagiarism.
allegation.title: Allegation

hearingDate.description: Scheduled hearing date/time. Required by FraudCaseHearingGuard before scheduleHearing can succeed.
hearingDate.title: Hearing Date

hearingRecords.description: Append-style record of held hearings — new entries are added, never overwritten.

attendees.title: Attendees
attendees.description: NC user IDs of everyone present at this hearing.

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Free-text notes recorded during or after this hearing.

evidenceRefs.title: Evidence References
evidenceRefs.description: References to evidence (e.g. OpenRegister file attachment identifiers) discussed at this hearing.

hearingRecords.title: Hearing Records

verdict.description: Exam-board verdict, set by decide. Required by FraudCaseDecisionGuard.
verdict.title: Verdict

decisionRationale.description: Exam-board rationale for the decision. Required by FraudCaseDecisionGuard.
decisionRationale.title: Decision Rationale

decidedBy.description: Nextcloud user ID of the exam-board member who decided the case.
decidedBy.title: Decided By

decidedAt.description: When the case was decided. Stamped by FraudCaseDecisionGuard on decide.
decidedAt.title: Decided At

sanctionType.description: Sanction imposed when verdict is fraud-proven. Required by FraudCaseDecisionGuard when verdict=fraud-proven.
sanctionType.title: Sanction Type

sanctionDurationMonths.description: Duration of the sanction in months, capped at 12 ("up to one-year exclusion"). Required by FraudCaseDecisionGuard when verdict=fraud-proven. The maximum is also schema-enforced (defense in depth) so a direct API write cannot bypass the guard's cap.
sanctionDurationMonths.title: Sanction Duration (Months)

sanctionScope.description: Scope of the sanction. Required by FraudCaseDecisionGuard when verdict=fraud-proven.
sanctionScope.title: Sanction Scope

appealDeadline.description: decidedAt + 42 days (the CBE appeal window). Stamped by FraudCaseDecisionGuard on decide — a guard side-effect, not a declarative calculation (this register's calculation DSL has no date-arithmetic primitive at HEAD).
appealDeadline.title: Appeal Deadline

appealLodged.description: Whether the accused has lodged a CBE appeal.
appealLodged.title: Appeal Lodged

appealOutcome.description: Outcome of the CBE appeal, if lodged. This spec records the outcome as a case fact only — it does not model the CBE's own adjudication process.
appealOutcome.title: Appeal Outcome

lifecycle.description: Case lifecycle state.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

subject.en: The fraud case has been decided

x-property-rbac._comment: Fraud dossiers are highly sensitive. Read access restricted to admin, examboard, the accused learner, and the reporter — fail-closed for everyone else. Hearing/decision internals are withheld from the accused/reporter by ExamCaseDossierView (UI convention, not field-level server RBAC — this register has no field-level RBAC primitive at HEAD).

groupPlanId.description: UUID of the GroupPlan this subgroup belongs to.

learnerIds.description: Nextcloud user IDs of the learners in this subgroup (same convention as Cohort.learnerIds). No learningPlanId/learningPlanIds field is stored here — a member's active LearningPlan is resolved live by learnerId lookup (GroupPlanSubgroupLearnerContext.vue).
learnerIds.title: Learner IDs

differentiatedGoal.description: The differentiated goal for this subgroup, distinct from the GroupPlan's group-wide goals.

approach.description: The instruction/aanpak used for this subgroup to reach its differentiated goal.

intendedOutcome.description: The outcome this subgroup's differentiated approach is intended to produce by period end.

SupportRequest.title: SupportRequest
SupportRequest.description: A zorgvraag (support/care request) raised from a pupil's dossier by a coordinator, signalling that a learner's needs may exceed what the school can provide alone. May precede a LearningPlan (OPP) or reference an existing one. Submitting it auto-queues a DataExchangeJob (target: swv) that routes an OSO-format care-request dossier to the regional samenwerkingsverband (SWV) for deliberation and a possible TlvApplication.

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner this support request concerns.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

learningPlanId.description: UUID of the LearningPlan (OPP) this request relates to. Nullable — a zorgvraag frequently precedes the OPP that follows from it, so the request must remain creatable without one.
learningPlanId.title: Learning Plan ID

originGroupPlanSubgroupId.description: UUID of the GroupPlanSubgroup this request was raised from, when a group-level differentiated approach proved insufficient for this learner. Nullable and independent of learningPlanId — a request may originate from a GroupPlanSubgroup, from an existing LearningPlan, from both, or from neither.
originGroupPlanSubgroupId.title: Origin Group Plan Subgroup ID

raisedBy.description: Nextcloud user ID of the coordinator/IB'er who raised this request — same convention as LearningPlan.coordinatorId.
raisedBy.title: Raised By

description.description: Narrative describing the support request.
description.title: Description

dataExchangeJobId.description: UUID of the DataExchangeJob (target: swv) auto-queued when this request is submitted. Null until submitted.
dataExchangeJobId.title: Data Exchange Job ID

lifecycle.description: Support-request lifecycle state.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

submit.description: Coordinator submits the request. Auto-queues a DataExchangeJob (target: swv, scope.schema: support-request) via SupportRequestSubmitHandler, which immediately advances the job into pending-parent-review.

routeToSwv.description: Set by DataExchangeRunHandler once the SWV DataExchangeJob succeeds — the dossier has been delivered.

x-openregister-authorization._comment: Zorg data is the most sensitive category this app handles (openspec/changes/zorgvraag-swv-tlv-chain/design.md Security Considerations). LearnerProfile.roles has no dedicated zorgcoördinator/coordinator role today — a fleet-wide role addition is out of scope for this change — so creation is restricted to admin/principal at the declarative RBAC layer, the tightest posture available without inventing a role. Coordinator-level authorship is expressed via the raisedBy NC-user-id convention (same as LearningPlan.coordinatorId) rather than a role gate; object-level enforcement of that convention at create time is a known platform gap, same as documented for secure-exam-test-mode (lib/Settings/scholiq_register.json:5105).

x-property-rbac._comment: Full-detail read restricted to admin/principal plus the coordinator who raised the request (raisedBy match), per design.md Security Considerations. A future portal-contribution provider MUST field-project parent/pupil reads rather than reuse this block — explicitly out of scope for this change.

subject.nl: De zorgvraag is doorgestuurd naar het samenwerkingsverband
subject.en: The support request was routed to the samenwerkingsverband

TlvApplication.title: TlvApplication
TlvApplication.description: The toelaatbaarheidsverklaring (TLV) application, tracing to the SupportRequest that triggered it. Scholiq records the samenwerkingsverband's (SWV) decision — it does not adjudicate it, per Wet passend onderwijs. validUntil drives a declared tlvExpiringSoon calculation (no PHP TimedJob).

supportRequestId.description: UUID of the SupportRequest this TLV application traces to. A TLV always traces to a SupportRequest.
supportRequestId.title: Support Request ID

swvCaseReference.description: The SWV's own case reference number, set once known.
swvCaseReference.title: SWV Case Reference

decision.description: The SWV's decision, recorded by a coordinator once received. Null until decided. Scholiq records this decision; it implements no logic that auto-approves, auto-rejects, or predicts it.
decision.title: Decision

validFrom.description: Start of the TLV validity period. Set only once the decision is recorded.
validFrom.title: Valid From

validUntil.description: End of the TLV validity period. Set only once the decision is recorded. Drives the declared tlvExpiringSoon calculation below.
validUntil.title: Valid Until

decisionDocumentRef.description: Reference to the OR file attachment carrying the SWV's decision document.
decisionDocumentRef.title: Decision Document Reference

lifecycle.description: TLV application lifecycle state.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

decide.description: Coordinator records the SWV's externally-issued decision (approved|rejected|conditional) plus validFrom/validUntil/decisionDocumentRef. No automated approval/rejection logic runs — the SWV is the sole deciding authority (design.md Decisions: 'TLV decision recorded, never adjudicated').

x-openregister-authorization._comment: Same tightest-posture rationale as SupportRequest (openspec/changes/zorgvraag-swv-tlv-chain/design.md Security Considerations) — zorg data, no dedicated coordinator role exists yet.

x-property-rbac._comment: Full-detail read restricted to admin/principal per design.md Security Considerations. TlvApplication carries no raisedBy-equivalent field of its own (it traces to SupportRequest via supportRequestId), so a per-object coordinator-match read rule cannot be expressed declaratively here without denormalising a field — deferred alongside the future portal-contribution provider design.md already scopes out of this change.

daysUntilValidUntil.description: Days remaining until validUntil. Null when validUntil is not set.

tlvExpiringSoon.description: True when an approved TLV's validity period is within 30 days of expiring (and not yet expired). Declared calculation trigger — no PHP TimedJob (design.md Decisions).

subject.nl: Er is een TLV-besluit ontvangen van het samenwerkingsverband
subject.en: A TLV decision was received from the samenwerkingsverband

DeliberationRecord.title: DeliberationRecord
DeliberationRecord.description: A structured, append-only record of a consultation round (parents, municipality, care partners, school, SWV coordinator) concerning a SupportRequest and/or TlvApplication. Carries a first-class pupilVoice block reflecting the 2025 Wet versterking positie ouders en leerlingen in passend onderwijs hoorrecht (insight 1145). Once recorded, immutable — a correction creates a new record referencing the one it supersedes rather than mutating it.

supportRequestId.description: UUID of the SupportRequest this deliberation concerns. At least one of supportRequestId/tlvApplicationId is required.
supportRequestId.title: Support Request ID

tlvApplicationId.description: UUID of the TlvApplication this deliberation concerns. At least one of supportRequestId/tlvApplicationId is required.
tlvApplicationId.title: TLV Application ID

attendees.description: Role-tagged attendees of this deliberation round.

role.description: The attendee's role in the deliberation.
role.title: Attendee Role

name.description: Free-text name, for attendees not backed by an NC user account (e.g. a municipality or care-partner representative).
name.title: Attendee Name

refId.description: NC user ID or LearnerProfile UUID, for attendees who are Scholiq subjects.
refId.title: Attendee Reference ID

attendees.title: Attendees

scheduledAt.description: ISO 8601 datetime the deliberation round was/is scheduled for.
scheduledAt.title: Scheduled At

recordedAt.description: ISO 8601 datetime the deliberation outcome was recorded. Null until recorded.
recordedAt.title: Recorded At

outcome.description: Recommendation / conclusion of the deliberation round.
outcome.title: Outcome

pupilVoice.description: The pupil's own voice, distinct from parent consent (2025 hoorrecht, insight 1145). A DeliberationRecord MUST NOT reach recorded unless heard is true or waived carries a non-empty waiverReason — enforced by PupilVoiceGuard.

heard.description: True when the pupil was directly heard.
heard.title: Pupil Heard

statementNote.description: The pupil's own recorded statement, distinct from any parent Signature/consent recorded elsewhere on the chain.
statementNote.title: Pupil Statement Note

waived.description: True when directly hearing the pupil is not appropriate (e.g. very young children).
waived.title: Hoorrecht Waived

waiverReason.description: Required, non-empty reason when waived is true. Enforced by PupilVoiceGuard at the scheduled → recorded transition.
waiverReason.title: Waiver Reason

pupilVoice.title: Pupil Voice

correctsId.description: UUID of the prior DeliberationRecord this record corrects. Set only when this record is a correction of an earlier appendOnly record — the original is never mutated.
correctsId.title: Corrects Record ID

lifecycle.description: Deliberation record lifecycle state. appendOnly: true once recorded.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

record.description: Blocked unless pupilVoice.heard is true or pupilVoice.waived is true with a non-empty waiverReason (2025 hoorrecht, insight 1145).

x-openregister-authorization._comment: Same tightest-posture rationale as SupportRequest (openspec/changes/zorgvraag-swv-tlv-chain/design.md Security Considerations).

x-property-rbac._comment: Full-detail read (including other attendees' coordinator/staff-only contributions) restricted to admin/principal per design.md Security Considerations. pupilVoice.statementNote MUST be pupil-visible where portal access exists — a future portal-contribution provider's field-projection concern, explicitly out of scope for this change (design.md).

x-openregister-processing.doelbinding: Record routine staff observations, conversations, and concerns about a learner as part of the school's ongoing pastoral/mentoring duty of care, distinct from the formal zorgvraag/OPP track.
x-openregister-processing.retentionReference: not declared (DossierNote carries no x-openregister-archival annotation; align with the school's selectielijst onderwijs)

DossierNote.title: DossierNote
DossierNote.description: The everyday pupil-dossier note a mentor/coordinator keeps day to day — an observation, a conversation, a phone call home, a concern, or a positive note — distinct from the formal OPP/zorgvraag track (LearningPlan/SupportRequest/DeliberationRecord). appendOnly: true (ADR-008) — a correction is a new record, never an in-place edit of prior evidence about a named minor. Server-side x-property-rbac restricts every row to admin/mentor/coordinator/the note's own author; the confidentiality field's finer three-way tiering beyond that floor is a named OpenRegister platform-capability gap (see openspec/changes/pupil-dossier-notes/design.md Decision 1) — NOT enforced per-row today.

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner this dossier note concerns.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

authorId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the staff member (mentor/coordinator/admin) who authored this note.
authorId.title: Author ID

date.description: Date the observed event (observation/conversation/phone call/concern) took place.
date.title: Date

category.description: The kind of everyday dossier entry this note records.
category.title: Category

confidentiality.description: The note's intended confidentiality tier. Presentational default only — the server-enforced read floor (admin/mentor/coordinator/author) is currently the SAME for all three values; see the schema-level x-property-rbac _comment and design.md Decision 1 for the named platform gap.
confidentiality.title: Confidentiality

x-openregister-authorization._comment: A learner or parent cannot author a DossierNote about anyone (design.md Security/Privacy Posture).

x-property-rbac._comment: Read restricted to admin/mentor/coordinator/the note's own authorId — the tightest common floor x-property-rbac can express (one static per-schema policy, uniformly applied to every row). This fails closed: no parent, pupil, or unrelated account can ever read a DossierNote regardless of its confidentiality value. The finer team-visible/care-team-only/private-to-author distinction WITHIN that floor is NOT enforced server-side today — a verified, named OpenRegister platform-capability gap (row-conditional x-property-rbac), not a fabricated guarantee. See openspec/changes/pupil-dossier-notes/design.md Decision 1.

x-openregister-processing.doelbinding: Record behaviour incidents involving a learner, their follow-up handling, and an optional escalation into a formal SupportRequest, as part of the school's duty of care.
x-openregister-processing.retentionReference: not declared (BehaviourIncident carries no x-openregister-archival annotation; align with the school's selectielijst onderwijs)

BehaviourIncident.title: BehaviourIncident
BehaviourIncident.description: A behaviour incident involving a learner — what/where/who-involved, severity, an append-only follow-up log, and an eventual resolution. Optionally references a SupportRequest (escalatedSupportRequestId) when staff raise a formal zorgvraag from it — the incident never duplicates SupportRequest fields, it only points at one. appendOnly: true (ADR-008); own lifecycle open -> in-handling -> resolved, independent of any linked SupportRequest's own lifecycle.

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner this incident concerns.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

reportedBy.description: Nextcloud user ID of the staff member (mentor/coordinator/admin) who reported this incident.
reportedBy.title: Reported By

location.description: Where the incident took place. Null when not recorded.
location.title: Location

involvedUserIds.description: Nextcloud user IDs of other learners/staff involved in the incident, if any. Null when not recorded.
involvedUserIds.title: Involved User IDs

followUpActions.description: Append-only log of the school's handling history for this incident (contact attempts, agreements reached, escalations). Each addition MUST be a new audited version of the appendOnly incident (ADR-008) — never an in-place edit of a prior entry. Same shape as AttendanceFlag.interventions.

recordedBy.description: Nextcloud user ID of the mentor/coordinator who recorded this follow-up action.
recordedBy.title: Recorded By

recordedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this follow-up action was recorded.
recordedAt.title: Recorded At

action.description: Free-text description of the follow-up action taken.
action.title: Action

followUpActions.title: Follow-Up Actions

resolution.description: How the incident was resolved. Set on the resolve transition. Null until resolved.
resolution.title: Resolution

escalatedSupportRequestId.description: UUID of the SupportRequest this incident escalated into, when staff raise a formal zorgvraag from it. Null unless escalated. A reference only — no SupportRequest field is duplicated onto BehaviourIncident.
escalatedSupportRequestId.title: Escalated Support Request ID

lifecycle.description: Behaviour-incident lifecycle state.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

x-openregister-authorization._comment: A learner or parent cannot author a BehaviourIncident about anyone (design.md Security/Privacy Posture).

x-property-rbac._comment: Read restricted to admin/mentor/coordinator/the incident's own reportedBy — same tightest-common-floor rationale as DossierNote (design.md Decision 1).

x-openregister-processing.doelbinding: Record a learner's own periodic self-reported mood/wellbeing signal, visible to their mentor, as part of the school's duty of care for pupil wellbeing.
x-openregister-processing.retentionReference: not declared (WellbeingCheckIn carries no x-openregister-archival annotation; align with the school's selectielijst onderwijs)
x-openregister-processing._comment: Open question for the privacy officer, not resolved by this schema: whether moodScale constitutes AVG Art. 9 special-category (health-adjacent) data requiring a stricter legal basis than public-task. Seeded as draft specifically so the privacy officer decides before activation (openspec/changes/pupil-dossier-notes/design.md Security/Privacy Posture).

WellbeingCheckIn.title: WellbeingCheckIn
WellbeingCheckIn.description: A light, learner-authored periodic wellbeing self-report — a mood scale plus an optional comment, visible to the learner's mentor/coordinator/admin and to the learner's own submissions (the Microsoft Teams Education 'Reflect' shape, not a clinical instrument). appendOnly: true (ADR-008); no lifecycle — each check-in is a single point-in-time record.

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner submitting this check-in.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

submittedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the check-in was submitted.
submittedAt.title: Submitted At

moodScale.description: Self-reported mood on a 1 (low) to 5 (high) scale.
moodScale.title: Mood Scale

comment.description: Optional free-text comment accompanying the mood scale. Null when not provided.
comment.title: Comment

x-property-rbac._comment: Read restricted to admin/mentor/coordinator/the submitting learner (self-read of one's own check-ins). The 'mentor' role check is the school-wide mentor NC group, not the learner's specifically assigned mentor (LearnerProfile.managerId) — x-property-rbac.match can only compare a field on the object itself against $userId, not against a related object's field, so scoping to 'this learner's specific mentor' is not expressible (same documented granularity limit as SupportRequest.raisedBy).

subject.nl: Er is een welbevinden check-in ingediend

x-openregister-processing.retentionReference: not declared (AttendanceRecord carries no x-openregister-archival annotation; align with the school's selectielijst onderwijs)

AttendanceRecord.title: AttendanceRecord
AttendanceRecord.description: Per-Session per-learner attendance fact. Records whether a learner was present, absent, late, or left early. Not appendOnly — a teacher may correct a mis-marked record; OR's update audit trail is sufficient.

sessionId.description: UUID of the Session this record belongs to.
sessionId.title: Session ID

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

learnerRef.description: UUID reference to the LearnerProfile object — the portal-subject scoping key (ADR-046 A4). Distinct from learnerId, which is the Nextcloud user id. Additive and optional; existing rows leave it unset and stay invisible to the portal (fail-closed) until backfilled.
learnerRef.title: Learner Ref

cohortId.description: Denormalised Cohort UUID for filtering. Derived from the Session.
cohortId.title: Cohort ID

minutesAttended.description: Actual minutes the learner was present. Null when fully absent.
minutesAttended.title: Minutes Attended

markedBy.description: Nextcloud user ID of the teacher or coordinator who marked attendance.
markedBy.title: Marked By

markedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when attendance was marked.
markedAt.title: Marked At

reason.description: Optional free-text reason note from the teacher.
reason.title: Absence Reason

excuseRequestId.description: UUID of the ExcuseRequest that flipped this record to absent-excused.
excuseRequestId.title: Excuse Request ID

lesuren.description: Derived lesuren count. When attended (present/late/left-early): minutesAttended / 60. When fully absent: session.durationMinutes / 60. Used for the 16-lesuur leerplicht threshold.

ExcuseRequest.title: ExcuseRequest
ExcuseRequest.description: Absence excuse submitted by a learner, parent, or 18+ learner-self for a date range. On approval, matching AttendanceRecords are flipped to absent-excused by ExcuseApprovalHandler.

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner whose absence is being excused.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

learnerRef.description: UUID reference to the LearnerProfile object whose absence is being excused — the portal-subject scoping key (ADR-046 A4). Distinct from learnerId, which is the Nextcloud user id. For a student self-submission the portal stamps this from the student subject; for a parent submission it is the child LearnerProfile UUID supplied on create. Additive and optional; existing rows leave it unset (fail-closed) until backfilled.
learnerRef.title: Learner Ref

submittedBy.description: Nextcloud user ID of the submitter (may be a parent or the 18+ learner themselves).
submittedBy.title: Submitted By

submittedByRef.description: UUID reference to the domain object that submitted this request (the guardian domain object for a parent submission, or the LearnerProfile for an 18+ self-submission) — the portal scope-stamp for parent-audience creates (ADR-046 A4). Distinct from submittedBy, which is the Nextcloud user id. Additive and optional; existing rows leave it unset.
submittedByRef.title: Submitted By Ref

dateFrom.description: First date of the absence period (inclusive, ISO 8601 date).
dateFrom.title: Absence From Date

dateTo.description: Last date of the absence period (inclusive, ISO 8601 date).
dateTo.title: Absence To Date

reasonKind.description: Structured reason category for reporting purposes.
reasonKind.title: Reason Category

attachmentRef.description: Optional reference to a supporting document (doctor's note, etc.).
attachmentRef.title: Attachment Reference

submittedAuthLevel.description: eIDAS assurance level of the submission flow. The DigiD handshake itself is out of scope; this field records the achieved level.
submittedAuthLevel.title: Submission Assurance Level

decidedBy.description: Nextcloud user ID of the coordinator who approved or rejected.
decidedBy.title: Decided By

decisionNote.description: Optional note from the coordinator explaining the decision.
decisionNote.title: Decision Note

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle state. Managed by OR lifecycle engine.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

AttendanceThreshold.title: AttendanceThreshold
AttendanceThreshold.description: Configurable threshold rule that watches learner attendance over a window and fires when the metric crosses the limit. The leerplicht-16uur kind is the Dutch Leerplichtwet art. 21a profile. Crossing detection uses the same calculatedChange mechanism as Regulation.ragStatus — NOT a TimedJob (ADR-022).

kind.description: Profile kind. leerplicht-16uur is the Leerplichtwet art. 21a profile (16 unexcused lesuren in 4 rolling weeks).
kind.title: Threshold Kind

scope.description: Whether the threshold applies per individual learner or aggregated per cohort.
scope.title: Threshold Scope

cohortId.description: UUID of the Cohort this threshold applies to. Null = applies to all cohorts in the tenant.
cohortId.title: Cohort ID

weeks.description: Number of rolling weeks (when type is rolling-weeks). E.g. 4 for the leerplicht 4-week window.
weeks.title: Rolling Weeks

metric.description: Metric being measured. unexcused-lesuren counts lesuren from AttendanceRecords with status absent-unexcused.
metric.title: Threshold Metric

limit.description: Threshold value. E.g. 16 for the leerplicht rule.
limit.title: Threshold Limit

lesuurMinutes.description: Minutes per lesuur, used when metric is unexcused-lesuren. Default 60.
lesuurMinutes.title: Minutes per Lesuur

onCross.description: Action to take when the threshold is crossed.

notify.description: Whether to send notifications to notifyRoles.
notify.title: Send Notification

notifyRoles.description: Tenant roles to notify on crossing (e.g. ["mentor", "coordinator"]).
notifyRoles.title: Notify Roles

createFlag.description: Whether to create an AttendanceFlag on crossing.
createFlag.title: Create Attendance Flag

dataExchangeTarget.title: Data Exchange Target

onCross.title: On Threshold Crossed

active.description: Whether this threshold rule is currently enforced.
active.title: Active

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle state. Managed by OR lifecycle engine.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

AttendanceFlag.description: Created when an AttendanceThreshold is crossed. appendOnly: true per ADR-008 (immutable audit evidence). The flag tracks the mentor's intervention and any outbound leerplicht report via its lifecycle. It NEVER auto-acts against the learner — human-in-the-loop throughout.
