PortfolioTemplate.title: PortfolioTemplate
PortfolioTemplate.description: Teacher-authored structure that pre-structures a course-bound Portfolio: ordered sections, each with criteria the learner must evidence, mirroring LearningPlanTemplate.sections's ordered-section shape combined with Rubric.criteria's criterion shape. An optional rubricId lets the governing teacher apply course-bound scoring when reviewing a Portfolio built from this template. Lifecycle: draft → active → archived (mirrors LearningPlanTemplate).

name.description: Human-readable template name, e.g. 'BPV kwalificatiedossier portfolio 2026'.
name.title: Template Name

kind.description: Portfolio flavour this template pre-structures — mirrors Portfolio.kind.
kind.title: Portfolio Kind

description.description: Optional longer description of the template's purpose and use.
description.title: Template Description

sections.description: Ordered sections a Portfolio built from this template must evidence.

order.description: Display order of this section within the template.
order.title: Section Order

helpText.description: Optional guidance text shown to the learner filling in this section.
helpText.title: Help Text

criteria.description: Criteria the learner must evidence within this section.

description.description: What the learner must evidence to satisfy this criterion.
description.title: Criterion Description

competencyId.description: UUID of the Competency (competency capability) this criterion evidences. competency-framework is merged at HEAD, so this is a real $ref rather than the free-text competencyCode/competencyLabel fields this change's spec assumed before that capability existed — see design.md.
competencyId.title: Competency ID

criteria.title: Section Criteria

sections.title: Portfolio Sections

rubricId.description: UUID of an optional Rubric the grading teacher applies when reviewing a course-bound Portfolio built from this template.
rubricId.title: Rubric ID

lifecycle.description: Template lifecycle state.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

Portfolio.title: Portfolio
Portfolio.description: The container a learner curates evidence into — references, never copies, an NC file, a Submission, a WerkprocesAssessment, an ExternalTrainingRecord, a Credential, or a free-text reflection via its PortfolioEntry rows. Two flavours share one schema via kind: personal (spans the whole study, never submitted for grading) and course-bound (instantiated from a PortfolioTemplate as a graded course task). A single x-openregister-lifecycle serves both flavours — a personal portfolio simply never calls submit/grade.

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner who owns this portfolio.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

learnerRef.description: UUID reference to the LearnerProfile object — the portal-subject scoping key (ADR-046 A4). Distinct from learnerId, which is the Nextcloud user id. Additive and optional; existing rows leave it unset and stay invisible to the portal (fail-closed) until backfilled.
learnerRef.title: Learner Ref

kind.description: Portfolio flavour. personal spans the whole study and is never submitted for grading; course-bound is instantiated from a PortfolioTemplate as a graded course task.
kind.title: Portfolio Kind

templateId.description: UUID of the governing PortfolioTemplate. Null for a personal portfolio, and for a course-bound portfolio a teacher assigned without a template.
templateId.title: Template ID

courseId.description: UUID of the Course this portfolio is a graded task for. Set only when kind=course-bound.
courseId.title: Course ID

curriculumPlanId.description: UUID of the CurriculumPlan this portfolio's grade contributes to. Set only when kind=course-bound.
curriculumPlanId.title: Curriculum Plan ID

curriculumPlanComponentId.description: Stable componentId from the parent CurriculumPlan.components array that this portfolio scores, mirroring Assignment.curriculumPlanComponentId. Set only when kind=course-bound.
curriculumPlanComponentId.title: Curriculum Plan Component ID

description.description: Optional longer description of this portfolio's purpose.
description.title: Description

dueAt.description: Optional due date for a course-bound portfolio's submission.
dueAt.title: Due At

gradeValue.description: Teacher-entered grade, mirrors Submission.proposedGrade. A portfolio's evidence is heterogeneous, so this is entered directly by the grading teacher rather than computed from a uniform rubric.
gradeValue.title: Grade Value

gradeEntryId.description: UUID of the GradeEntry created by PortfolioGradeEmitHandler when this portfolio transitions to graded. Read-only from this schema's perspective.
gradeEntryId.title: Grade Entry ID

lifecycle.description: Portfolio lifecycle state. Covers both flavours: personal uses draft/active/archived only; course-bound additionally uses submitted/graded.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

x-property-rbac._comment: eportfolio: a portfolio holds a learner's own evidence — mirrors Submission/AssessmentResult exactly. Learner sees own portfolios; admins see all. Portal/share-derived teacher/praktijkopleider/external-assessor reads are served by PortalContributionProvider and NC Files sharing, not by widening this block (design.md Security/RBAC Posture).

PortfolioEntry.title: PortfolioEntry
PortfolioEntry.description: One evidence item in a Portfolio. References — never copies — exactly one existing object per evidenceKind via its own nullable, typed $ref field (the GradeEntry.sourceKind per-kind-field shape), or carries a free-text reflection. No lifecycle of its own — a plain child record.

portfolioId.description: UUID of the Portfolio this entry belongs to.
portfolioId.title: Portfolio ID

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner this entry belongs to, denormalized from the parent Portfolio.learnerId for RBAC/query convenience — mirrors SelfAssessment's own learnerId field, which duplicates data also reachable via its submissionId parent link.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

title.description: Human-readable title of this evidence item.
title.title: Title

evidenceKind.description: The kind of evidence this entry carries. Exactly one of the per-kind fields below resolves the referenced object for every kind except reflection, which carries reflectionText only.
evidenceKind.title: Evidence Kind

attachmentRef.description: OpenRegister file attachment reference (nc:files path or OR attachment ID), mirroring Submission.attachmentRefs's untyped NC-file shape. The app does NOT store file bytes. Set when evidenceKind=file.
attachmentRef.title: Attachment Reference

submissionId.description: UUID of the referenced Submission. Set when evidenceKind=submission.
submissionId.title: Submission ID

werkprocesAssessmentId.description: UUID of the referenced WerkprocesAssessment. Set when evidenceKind=werkproces-assessment.
werkprocesAssessmentId.title: Werkproces Assessment ID

externalTrainingRecordId.description: UUID of the referenced ExternalTrainingRecord. Set when evidenceKind=external-training-record.
externalTrainingRecordId.title: External Training Record ID

credentialId.description: UUID of the referenced Credential. Set when evidenceKind=credential.
credentialId.title: Credential ID

reflectionText.description: The learner's own reflection text. Required when evidenceKind=reflection; may also accompany any other kind.
reflectionText.title: Reflection Text

sectionId.description: Matches the governing PortfolioTemplate.sections[].sectionId this entry evidences, when the parent Portfolio is templated.
sectionId.title: Section ID

criterionId.description: Matches the governing PortfolioTemplate.sections[].criteria[].criterionId this entry evidences, when the parent Portfolio is templated.
criterionId.title: Criterion ID

competencyId.description: UUID of the Competency (competency capability) this entry evidences. competency-framework is merged at HEAD, so this is a real $ref rather than the free-text competencyCode/competencyLabel fields this change's spec assumed before that capability existed — see design.md.
competencyId.title: Competency ID

allOf._comment: eportfolio: reflectionText is required when evidenceKind=reflection (the entry's sole content in that case) and optional alongside any other kind. Mirrors GradeEntry's if/then conditional-requiredness-by-discriminator shape.

x-property-rbac._comment: eportfolio: mirrors SelfAssessment's learner-self pattern via the denormalized learnerId field above. Learner sees own entries; admins see all. Portal/share-derived reads are served by PortalContributionProvider and NC Files sharing, not by widening this block.

ExternalAssessor.title: ExternalAssessor
ExternalAssessor.description: A non-BPV external assessor granted read-only portfolio access. A plain reference/identity object with no OpenRegister lifecycle — a person with no Nextcloud account by definition, structurally identical to Praktijkopleider (ADR-046 premise). This object's own UUID is the portal identity anchor. `active` alone gates whether the portal identity is usable.

givenName.description: Given name of the external assessor.
givenName.title: Given Name

familyName.description: Family name of the external assessor.
familyName.title: Family Name

email.description: Contact email address of the external assessor — used for out-of-band (non-Scholiq) communication, since no NC-reachable notification channel exists for this role.
email.title: Email Address

organisationName.description: Name of the organisation the external assessor represents, if any.
organisationName.title: Organisation Name

active.description: Whether this external assessor's portal identity is currently usable. False retires the identity without deleting the audit trail.
active.title: Active

PortfolioShare.title: PortfolioShare
PortfolioShare.description: A single, revocable, read-only grant of a learner's Portfolio (or a selection of its PortfolioEntry rows) to a teacher, a BPV praktijkopleider, or an external assessor. Append-only audit trail of who was granted access and when (lifecycle: draft → active → revoked). On grant (draft → active), PortfolioShareGrantHandler creates a native Nextcloud Files share for sharedWithKind=teacher; praktijkopleider/external-assessor visibility is served declaratively by PortalContributionProvider.

portfolioId.description: UUID of the Portfolio being shared.
portfolioId.title: Portfolio ID

entryIds.description: UUIDs of a selection of the same Portfolio's own PortfolioEntry rows (not a copy). Null means the whole portfolio.
entryIds.title: Entry IDs

sharedWithKind.description: Which kind of recipient this grant targets. Exactly one of the per-kind reference fields below is set.
sharedWithKind.title: Shared With Kind

sharedWithTeacherId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the teacher recipient. Set when sharedWithKind=teacher.
sharedWithTeacherId.title: Shared With Teacher ID

sharedWithPraktijkopleiderId.description: UUID of the recipient Praktijkopleider. Set when sharedWithKind=praktijkopleider.
sharedWithPraktijkopleiderId.title: Shared With Praktijkopleider ID

sharedWithExternalAssessorId.description: UUID of the recipient ExternalAssessor. Set when sharedWithKind=external-assessor.
sharedWithExternalAssessorId.title: Shared With External Assessor ID

sharedBy.description: Nextcloud user ID of the person who created this grant (the portfolio's own learner, or admin/teacher-role staff). Never equal to the resolved recipient identity — enforced by PortfolioShareGrantHandler's guard.
sharedBy.title: Shared By

expiresAt.description: Optional expiry timestamp after which the grant is no longer honoured.
expiresAt.title: Expires At

lifecycle.description: Grant lifecycle state.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

PointRule.title: PointRule
PointRule.description: Declarative event -> points config for the engagement layer. Each kind maps to a real, already-firing HEAD transition -- no invented event sources: enrolment-completed fires on Enrolment -> completed (the transition XapiCompletionHandler already dispatches for mandatory-training completion); submission-on-time fires on Submission -> submitted where the materialised isLate calculation is false; finalgrade-passed fires on GradeEntry -> published/republish where GradeFormulaEvaluator::evaluate() returns passed:true; streak-milestone fires a bonus award when a learner's currentStreakDays newly crosses milestoneDays. Mirrors AttendanceThreshold's config/rule shape (draft -> active -> archived). No x-openregister-authorization block, same as AttendanceThreshold -- creation is gated by the same app-wide admin/coordinator posture every other config object in this register relies on.

name.description: Human-readable rule name, e.g. "On-time submission bonus".
name.title: Rule Name

kind.description: Which real HEAD event this rule reacts to. enrolment-completed: Enrolment -> completed. submission-on-time: Submission -> submitted with isLate false. finalgrade-passed: GradeEntry -> published/republish with GradeFormulaEvaluator passed:true. streak-milestone: a learner's currentStreakDays newly crosses milestoneDays. No peer-review kind exists -- peer review/peer grading is not implemented in the assignments capability.
kind.title: Rule Kind

points.description: Flat points awarded per firing. Copied onto each PointAward at award time -- editing this value never reprices existing awards.
points.title: Points

milestoneDays.description: Only meaningful for kind: streak-milestone -- the consecutive-day streak length that triggers the bonus award (e.g. 7, 30). Null for every other kind.
milestoneDays.title: Milestone Days

scope.description: Optional restriction to a specific Cohort or Course. Null means the rule applies tenant-wide.

cohortId.description: UUID of the Cohort this rule is scoped to. Null if scoped by course or tenant-wide.
cohortId.title: Cohort ID

courseId.description: UUID of the Course this rule is scoped to. Null if scoped by cohort or tenant-wide.
courseId.title: Course ID

scope.title: Scope

active.description: Whether this rule is currently enforced.
active.title: Active

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle state. Managed by OR lifecycle engine.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

PointAward.title: PointAward
PointAward.description: Append-only ledger row: one award of points to one learner for one firing of one PointRule. appendOnly: true per ADR-008 (immutable audit evidence), mirroring AttendanceFlag/AssessmentResult. Created only by PointAwardTriggerHandler / LearnerEngagementRollupHandler -- never directly by a learner, so a learner cannot retroactively pad their own score. points is copied from PointRule.points at award time and is never recomputed when the rule is later edited.

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner who earned the points.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

pointRuleId.description: UUID of the PointRule that fired.
pointRuleId.title: Point Rule ID

points.description: Points awarded, copied from PointRule.points at the moment of award. A later edit to the rule's points value does not retroactively reprice this award.
points.title: Points

sourceKind.description: Which kind of source object triggered this award.
sourceKind.title: Source Kind

sourceObjectId.description: Identifies the Enrolment/Submission/GradeEntry (via its curriculumPlanId) that fired the rule, per sourceKind. The target schema varies by sourceKind (polymorphic), so no single $ref applies -- the fleet's relation-dialect gate bans polymorphic $ref targets, same reasoning documented on PokSignature's description. Null for sourceKind: streak-milestone, which has no single triggering object.
sourceObjectId.title: Source Object ID

awardedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the award was created.
awardedAt.title: Awarded At

EngagementLevel.title: EngagementLevel
EngagementLevel.description: Tenant-scoped, admin-configurable engagement level threshold row (e.g. "Bronze", "Silver"). One row per level -- no lifecycle needed, a level either exists or is deleted, mirroring AttendanceThreshold's row-per-rule editability rather than a buried array a coordinator would have to edit in-place (compare GradeScale.bands[]).

order.description: Ascending display/comparison order among this tenant's levels.
order.title: Order

minPoints.description: Inclusive totalPoints threshold a learner must reach to hold this level.
minPoints.title: Minimum Points

icon.description: Material Design Icons name, matching every other schema's icon convention.
icon.title: Icon

LearnerEngagement.title: LearnerEngagement
LearnerEngagement.description: Calculated per-learner engagement roll-up -- totalPoints, resolved levelId, and streak days. x-openregister readOnly: true (mirrors FinalGrade exactly) so it can never be written directly by the frontend. One row per (learnerId, tenant_id). Recomputed by LearnerEngagementRollupHandler via PointEngagementEvaluator whenever a PointAward is created -- NOT a declarative x-openregister-aggregations sum metric, since no sum metric is used anywhere else in this register (only count/count_distinct are precedented).

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner this roll-up belongs to.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

totalPoints.description: Sum of the learner's PointAward.points, computed by PointEngagementEvaluator. Derived -- do not set manually.
totalPoints.title: Total Points

levelId.description: UUID of the highest EngagementLevel whose minPoints is at most totalPoints. Null before any level threshold is reached. Derived -- do not set manually.
levelId.title: Level ID

currentStreakDays.description: Consecutive calendar days (ending today or yesterday) with at least one PointAward. Derived -- do not set manually.
currentStreakDays.title: Current Streak Days

longestStreakDays.description: Historical maximum of currentStreakDays. Derived -- do not set manually.
longestStreakDays.title: Longest Streak Days

lastActivityDate.description: Date of the learner's most recent PointAward.awardedAt. Derived -- do not set manually.
lastActivityDate.title: Last Activity Date

lastRecomputedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last recompute triggered by a PointAward creation. Mirrors FinalGrade.lastRecomputedAt.
lastRecomputedAt.title: Last Recomputed At

calculatedChange.description: Recompute this LearnerEngagement whenever a PointAward for this learner is created. Implemented via LearnerEngagementRollupHandler listening on ObjectCreatedEvent -- NOT a TimedJob.

x-property-rbac._comment: engagement-gamification: a learner's points/level/streak are personal progress data. Learner sees own row via self-match; admins see all. Deliberately NOT opened up to cohort-mates -- see design.md 'Why the RBAC gap forces a controller'. Peer-visible rankings go through LeaderboardController instead, which additionally respects the per-learner leaderboard opt-out preference this self-match RBAC rule is independent of.

Leaderboard.title: Leaderboard
Leaderboard.description: Opt-in policy row scoping a ranked, peer-visible view of learner points to one Cohort or Course. Default OFF by construction: no Leaderboard row exists until a coordinator/admin explicitly creates and activates one for a specific cohort or course (x-openregister-seed is empty -- a fresh tenant has zero Leaderboard rows). Archiving one instantly turns that leaderboard back off. No ranking data is stored on this object itself -- LeaderboardController computes the ranking live so it always reflects the current per-learner opt-out state (see design.md).

cohortId.description: UUID of the Cohort this leaderboard ranks. Mutually exclusive with courseId, mirroring FinalGrade's courseId/programmeId dual-scope shape.
cohortId.title: Cohort ID

courseId.description: UUID of the Course this leaderboard ranks. Mutually exclusive with cohortId.
courseId.title: Course ID

topN.description: Display-limit hint for the frontend. Null means show all non-opted-out members.
topN.title: Top N

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle state. Managed by OR lifecycle engine. Only an active Leaderboard row is served by LeaderboardController -- draft and archived rows never produce a ranking.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

EvaluationCampaign.title: EvaluationCampaign
EvaluationCampaign.description: A course/module evaluation cycle (vakevaluatie): which Course(s)/Cohort(s) it covers, the academic period, the survey instrument, and a fixed anonymity policy. Opening a campaign provisions one EvaluationInvitation per learner in scope (EvaluationInvitationProvisioningHandler). Lifecycle: draft -> open -> closed -> archived. No existing course-management schema is modified -- courseIds/cohortIds reference Course/Cohort by $ref only. See openspec/changes/course-evaluation/.

closesAt.description: Date/time this campaign's evaluation window closes. Source of EvaluationInvitation.campaignClosesAt (stamped onto each invitation at open-time by EvaluationInvitationProvisioningHandler) — the reminder's scheduled+filter rule reads that denormalised copy, same shape as Enrolment.dueReminder reading the object's own dueDate.
closesAt.title: Closes At

courseIds.description: UUIDs of the Courses this campaign evaluates. At least one of courseIds/cohortIds MUST be populated (documented constraint, not a JSON-Schema conditional, matching this register's existing convention for cross-field constraints).
courseIds.title: Course IDs

cohortIds.description: UUIDs of the Cohorts this campaign evaluates. At least one of courseIds/cohortIds MUST be populated (documented constraint, not a JSON-Schema conditional).
cohortIds.title: Cohort IDs

academicYear.description: Academic or training year in YYYY or YYYY-YYYY format, same convention as Cohort.academicYear.
academicYear.title: Academic Year

period.description: Human-readable period label, same convention as Cohort.period.
period.title: Period Label

instrumentKind.description: built-in: Scholiq owns the questions[] instrument end-to-end. external-form: learners are pointed at a supplementary Nextcloud Forms survey via externalFormUrl for free-text-only qualitative input that Scholiq does NOT ingest, read, or aggregate -- a convenience link only (design.md Decision 1).
instrumentKind.title: Instrument Kind

questions.description: The built-in instrument's questions, in display order. Only meaningful when instrumentKind is built-in.

questionId.title: Question ID
questionId.description: Stable identifier for this question within the campaign, referenced by CourseEvaluationResponse.answers[].questionId.

text.title: Question Text
text.description: Localised question text, same nl/en shape as this register's notification subject fields.

kind.title: Question Kind
kind.description: likert-5: a 1-5 rating answer. free-text: an open free-text answer.

required.title: Required
required.description: Whether a learner must answer this question before submitting.

questions.title: Questions

externalFormUrl.description: URL of the supplementary Nextcloud Forms survey learners are pointed at. Only meaningful when instrumentKind is external-form. Scholiq never reads or aggregates what is submitted through it (design.md Decision 1).
externalFormUrl.title: External Form URL

anonymityPolicy.description: Documentation of the platform invariant that CourseEvaluationResponse carries no learner-identifying field -- a fixed value, not a per-campaign configurable toggle (design.md Decision 2).
anonymityPolicy.title: Anonymity Policy

reminderSchedule.description: Reminder configuration. leadDays is currently informational only -- v1 ships a single fixed P5D EvaluationInvitation.reminder rule (design.md Caveats); making leadDays actually drive that rule is a follow-up.
reminderSchedule.title: Reminder Schedule

enabled.title: Reminders Enabled
enabled.description: Whether non-responder reminders are sent for this campaign.

leadDays.title: Lead Days
leadDays.description: Intended days-before-close lead time for reminders. Informational only in v1 -- the declarative EvaluationInvitation.reminder rule uses a fixed P5D window regardless of this value (design.md Caveats).

lifecycle.description: Campaign lifecycle state. Managed by the OR lifecycle engine. Opening provisions EvaluationInvitations (EvaluationInvitationProvisioningHandler).
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

invitationProvisioning.description: Provision one EvaluationInvitation per learner in scope (resolved from courseIds/cohortIds via the referenced Cohort.learnerIds) when the campaign opens. Implemented via EvaluationInvitationProvisioningHandler listening on ObjectTransitionedEvent -- NOT a TimedJob. Idempotency-keyed so a duplicate open event does not create duplicate invitations for the same (campaignId, learnerId).

EvaluationInvitation.title: EvaluationInvitation
EvaluationInvitation.description: One system-provisioned row per learner per EvaluationCampaign, created when the campaign opens (EvaluationInvitationProvisioningHandler). The ONLY place a learner's identity and their evaluation-response status co-exist -- it never references which CourseEvaluationResponse satisfied it (no responseId field). CourseEvaluationEligibilityGuard and CourseEvaluationResponseSubmittedHandler are the only code paths that read/write it, both resolving the caller transiently via IUserSession (design.md Decision 2). System-provisioned -- no user-initiated lifecycle, no dedicated authoring UI.

campaignId.description: UUID of the EvaluationCampaign this invitation was provisioned for.
campaignId.title: Campaign ID

courseId.description: UUID of the Course this invitation's evaluation is about.
courseId.title: Course ID

cohortId.description: UUID of the Cohort this invitation was provisioned from. Null when the campaign scoped the learner directly via courseIds without a Cohort.
cohortId.title: Cohort ID

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the invited learner. Resolved transiently via IUserSession by the guard/handler pair -- never read from a client-supplied claim.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

hasResponded.description: Whether this learner has submitted a CourseEvaluationResponse for the campaign. Flipped to true by CourseEvaluationResponseSubmittedHandler after a successful submit -- never read back from, or linked to, the response itself.
hasResponded.title: Has Responded

respondedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when hasResponded was flipped to true. Null while unanswered.
respondedAt.title: Responded At

campaignClosesAt.description: Denormalised copy of the campaign's close date, stamped at provisioning time, so the reminder's scheduled+filter rule is self-contained -- same shape as Enrolment.dueReminder's use of the object's own dueDate.
campaignClosesAt.title: Campaign Closes At

academicYear.description: Denormalised copy of the campaign's academicYear, stamped at provisioning time, scoping this invitation into the same period CourseQualityScore aggregates by.
academicYear.title: Academic Year

period.description: Denormalised copy of the campaign's period, stamped at provisioning time, scoping this invitation into the same period CourseQualityScore aggregates by.
period.title: Period Label

subject.nl: Vergeet niet je vakevaluatie in te vullen
subject.en: Don't forget to complete your course evaluation

CourseEvaluationResponse.title: CourseEvaluationResponse
CourseEvaluationResponse.description: An anonymous course/module evaluation response, appendOnly. MUST NOT declare any property identifying the responding learner (no learnerId/submittedBy/equivalent) -- anonymity is enforced by the absence of such a property in the schema, not by an x-property-rbac rule hiding an existing one (design.md Decision 2). The draft -> submitted transition requires CourseEvaluationEligibilityGuard, which resolves the caller's identity server-side via IUserSession and never writes it onto this object.

campaignId.description: UUID of the EvaluationCampaign this response was submitted for.
campaignId.title: Campaign ID

courseId.description: UUID of the Course this response is about.
courseId.title: Course ID

cohortId.description: UUID of the Cohort this response's campaign scope came from. Null when the campaign scoped the response directly via courseIds without a Cohort.
cohortId.title: Cohort ID

teacherId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the teacher this response evaluates, for teacher-scoped evaluations. Null for course-only evaluations. NOT the responding learner's identity.
teacherId.title: Teacher ID

academicYear.description: Denormalised copy of the campaign's academicYear, so CourseQualityScoreEvaluator can scope its per-(course,teacher,academicYear,period) roll-up without joining back through EvaluationCampaign for every response.
academicYear.title: Academic Year

period.description: Denormalised copy of the campaign's period, same reasoning as academicYear.
period.title: Period Label

overallScore.description: Denormalised copy of the designated 'overall satisfaction' likert-5 answer from answers[], trading a small amount of redundancy for a materially simpler CourseQualityScoreEvaluator that averages a flat numeric column instead of parsing answers[] per response (design.md).
overallScore.title: Overall Score

answers.description: One entry per question answered, matching the campaign's questions[]. Never carries any field identifying the responding learner.

ratingValue.title: Rating Value
ratingValue.description: 1-5 answer for a likert-5 question. Null for a free-text question.

textValue.title: Text Value
textValue.description: Free-text answer for a free-text question. Null for a likert-5 question.

answers.title: Answers

submittedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the response transitioned to submitted. Null while in draft.
submittedAt.title: Submitted At

lifecycle.description: Response lifecycle state. Managed by the OR lifecycle engine. submitted is terminal (appendOnly).
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

qualityScoreRollup.description: Recompute the matching CourseQualityScore row (find-or-create, keyed on courseId/teacherId/academicYear/period) whenever a CourseEvaluationResponse submits. Implemented via CourseQualityScoreRollupHandler (internally calling CourseQualityScoreEvaluator) listening on ObjectTransitionedEvent -- NOT a TimedJob. Mirrors FinalGrade's own x-openregister-triggers block.

invitationFlip.description: Flip the submitting learner's own EvaluationInvitation to hasResponded:true/respondedAt:now, re-resolving the same session-caller identity the guard used. Implemented via CourseEvaluationResponseSubmittedHandler listening on ObjectTransitionedEvent -- never reads or writes an identity field on this object.

CourseQualityScore.title: CourseQualityScore
CourseQualityScore.description: Materialised roll-up per (courseId, teacherId nullable, academicYear, period), recomputed by CourseQualityScoreRollupHandler whenever a CourseEvaluationResponse for the matching scope submits, via CourseQualityScoreEvaluator (averaging is beyond this register's proven declarative aggregation metrics -- a full-file grep for "metric": shows only count/count_distinct in use anywhere in this register). No lifecycle -- fully derived, read-only from the frontend. Surfaced on the CourseQualityReport.vue custom view.

courseId.description: UUID of the Course this quality score is about.
courseId.title: Course ID

teacherId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the teacher this quality score is about, for teacher-scoped evaluations. Null for course-level-only scores.
teacherId.title: Teacher ID

academicYear.description: Academic year in YYYY or YYYY-YYYY format, matching the source responses' academicYear.
academicYear.title: Academic Year

period.description: Period label, matching the source responses' period.
period.title: Period Label

responseCount.description: Count of submitted CourseEvaluationResponses in this scope. Derived -- do not set manually.
responseCount.title: Response Count

invitationCount.description: Count of EvaluationInvitations provisioned in this scope. Derived -- do not set manually.
invitationCount.title: Invitation Count

averageOverallScore.description: Mean of overallScore across the scope's submitted responses (null values ignored, not treated as 0). Null before any response has submitted. Derived -- do not set manually.
averageOverallScore.title: Average Overall Score

responseRate.description: responseCount / invitationCount, 0 when invitationCount is 0 (never a divide-by-zero error). Derived -- do not set manually.
responseRate.title: Response Rate

lastRecomputedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last recompute triggered by a CourseEvaluationResponse submit.
lastRecomputedAt.title: Last Recomputed At

responseCountAggregate.description: Cross-schema aggregation: number of submitted CourseEvaluationResponses for this (courseId, teacherId, academicYear, period) scope. Documentation of the count CourseQualityScoreEvaluator computes in PHP -- like FinalGrade.publishedEntries, this does not itself materialise the field; CourseQualityScoreRollupHandler writes responseCount directly.

invitationCountAggregate.description: Cross-schema aggregation: number of EvaluationInvitations provisioned for this (courseId, academicYear, period) scope. Documentation only, same reasoning as responseCountAggregate.

ImprovementAction.title: ImprovementAction
ImprovementAction.description: Closes the evaluation cycle: an opleidingscommissie/vaksectie reviewer's findings and a resulting recorded action against a campaign/course's results, with a targetPeriod naming when the action should show results. Purely declarative CRUD -- no PHP guard, matching the RolloverPlan/CatalogChangeRequest-style housekeeping-tracker shape already used elsewhere in this register.

campaignId.description: UUID of the EvaluationCampaign this action responds to.
campaignId.title: Campaign ID

courseId.description: UUID of the Course this action targets.
courseId.title: Course ID

reviewedBy.description: Nextcloud user ID of the governance reviewer (opleidingscommissie/vaksectie) who recorded this action.
reviewedBy.title: Reviewed By

findings.description: The reviewer's findings from the evaluation results.
findings.title: Findings

actionDescription.description: The resulting improvement action to be taken.
actionDescription.title: Action Description

targetPeriod.description: The next period this action should show results in, same free-text convention as Cohort.period.
targetPeriod.title: Target Period

lifecycle.description: Action lifecycle state. Managed by the OR lifecycle engine.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

FeeItem.title: FeeItem
FeeItem.description: The chargeable definition: what is being charged for (course enrolment, school trip, materials, schoolkassa contribution, MBO contractonderwijs), its amount/currency/tax posture, and a required voluntary flag. voluntary: true is the Wet vrijwillige ouderbijdrage case (schoolkassa, trips, materials a school chooses to make voluntary) — FeeItemVoluntaryEntitlementGuard structurally blocks any Entitlement referencing a voluntary FeeItem from ever activating. voluntary: false is a genuine paid product (contractonderwijs, a paid course) that may legitimately gate access via Entitlement/EntitlementOrderPaidGuard. See openspec/changes/school-payments/.

name.description: Human-readable name of the chargeable, e.g. 'School trip to the Ardennes' or 'MBO Contractonderwijs — Excel Basics'.
name.title: Fee Item Name

description.description: Optional longer description of what this fee covers.
description.title: Description

kind.description: What kind of chargeable this is. Drives which linkedCourseId/linkedCohortId is relevant.
kind.title: Fee Kind

amount.description: The amount charged per unit, in the given currency.
amount.title: Amount

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code. Defaults to EUR.
currency.title: Currency

voluntary.description: REQUIRED legal fork point (Wet vrijwillige ouderbijdrage, in force since 1 August 2021). true = a voluntary parental contribution (schoolkassa, school trip, materials) an author must opt into explicitly — non-payment MUST NOT exclude a pupil from the activity it funds, and FeeItemVoluntaryEntitlementGuard structurally enforces this by blocking any Entitlement referencing this FeeItem from ever reaching active. false (the schema default) = a genuine paid product (MBO contractonderwijs, a paid course) that may legitimately gate access.
voluntary.title: Voluntary Contribution

taxPosture.description: Descriptive metadata only — NOT a computed VAT/BTW engine. An invoice-generation step downstream could read this; this schema computes no tax.
taxPosture.title: Tax Posture

linkedCourseId.description: UUID of the Course this fee is for, when kind is course-enrolment or mbo-contractonderwijs. Null for kinds with no direct course link.
linkedCourseId.title: Linked Course ID

linkedCohortId.description: UUID of the Cohort this fee is for, when kind is school-trip. Null for kinds with no direct cohort link.
linkedCohortId.title: Linked Cohort ID

academicYear.description: Free-text academic year this fee applies to, e.g. '2026-2027'.
academicYear.title: Academic Year

validFrom.description: ISO date from which this fee may be charged. Null means no start restriction.
validFrom.title: Valid From

validUntil.description: ISO date after which this fee may no longer be charged. Null means no end restriction.
validUntil.title: Valid Until

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle state. Managed by the OR lifecycle engine; do not set directly.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

Order.title: Order
Order.description: The payer-facing request for payment: payer (guardian/learner/employer), beneficiary learner, total, currency, due date, and a lifecycle draft -> open -> partially-paid | paid, draft | open -> cancelled, paid -> refunded. isOverdue is a materialised calculation using the identical @now-comparison idiom Enrolment.isOverdue already uses; dueSoon/overdue reuse Enrolment.dueReminder/Enrolment.overdue's exact x-openregister-notifications shape. See openspec/changes/school-payments/.

payerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the payer, when the payer has an NC account (guardian or learner paying for themselves).
payerId.title: Payer ID

payerName.description: Free-text payer name, for an employer or other payer with no Nextcloud account.
payerName.title: Payer Name

payerEmail.description: Payer email address, for an employer or other payer with no Nextcloud account, or as a notification fallback.
payerEmail.title: Payer Email

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the beneficiary learner — who this Order's chargeables are for.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

learnerRef.description: UUID reference to the LearnerProfile object — the portal-subject scoping key (ADR-046 A4). Distinct from learnerId, which is the Nextcloud user id. Additive and optional; existing rows leave it unset and stay invisible to the portal (fail-closed) until backfilled.
learnerRef.title: Learner Ref

totalAmount.description: The total amount owed for this Order — the sum of its OrderLines. Validated (not merely trusted) by OrderTotalValidationGuard at the draft -> open transition.
totalAmount.title: Total Amount

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code. Defaults to EUR.
currency.title: Currency

dueDate.description: ISO date by which payment is due. Null means no deadline.
dueDate.title: Due Date

lifecycle.description: Order lifecycle state. Managed by the OR lifecycle engine; do not set directly.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

x-openregister-lifecycle._comment: markPartiallyPaid/markPaid are driven by PaymentTransactionStatusHandler (event-driven, ADR-022 — NOT a TimedJob) reacting to a PaymentTransaction reaching succeeded, never called directly by a controller. refund is driven by the same handler reacting to a PaymentTransaction reaching refunded.

subject.nl: Je betaling moet binnenkort voldaan zijn

x-property-rbac._comment: Mirrors FinalGrade's block: the payer (payerId) and the beneficiary learner (learnerId) read their own Order; admin/finance roles read all. A learner/guardian MUST NOT read another payer's Order.

OrderLine.title: OrderLine
OrderLine.description: One priced line on an Order, referencing a FeeItem. A snapshot child row (mirrors AttendanceRecord/GradeEntry's shape), not an inline array — no verified precedent in this register for an array-of-composite-objects property carrying its own $ref relations. Order.totalAmount is validated against the sum of its OrderLines' lineTotal by OrderTotalValidationGuard at the draft -> open transition. See openspec/changes/school-payments/.

orderId.description: UUID of the parent Order this line belongs to.
orderId.title: Order ID

feeItemId.description: UUID of the FeeItem this line charges for.
feeItemId.title: Fee Item ID

description.description: Snapshot of the FeeItem's name at the time this line was added — kept stable even if the FeeItem is later edited.
description.title: Line Description

unitAmount.description: Amount charged per unit, in the parent Order's currency.
unitAmount.title: Unit Amount

lineTotal.description: quantity * unitAmount. Summed across all of an Order's OrderLines by OrderTotalValidationGuard and compared against Order.totalAmount.
lineTotal.title: Line Total

PaymentTransaction.title: PaymentTransaction
PaymentTransaction.description: One PSP round-trip against an Order (pending -> awaiting-redirect -> succeeded | failed | expired | cancelled, succeeded -> refunded). Append-only per ADR-008, mirroring Attestation's evidentiary shape. Records only the opaque status OpenConnector's (not-yet-built) PSP adapter returns — scholiq never parses a Mollie/Stripe-specific payload field, matching the 'forward the response as-is' posture LtiToolPlacementController already establishes for LTI. See openspec/changes/school-payments/.

orderId.description: UUID of the Order this payment attempt is for.
orderId.title: Order ID

pspProvider.description: Which payment service provider this transaction routes through — matches the credential-broker's catalogued mollie/stripe provider identifiers (openregister/lib/Settings/credential-providers.json).
pspProvider.title: PSP Provider

pspPaymentId.description: Opaque payment identifier returned by OpenConnector's PSP adapter once initiation succeeds. Scholiq stores this value only — never inspects or parses it. KNOWN GAP (documented, not fabricated): this schema is appendOnly (OpenRegister rejects any UPDATE, INSERT only), and this value is only knowable AFTER the PaymentTransaction row already exists (the row's own id is needed as the callback reference sent to OpenConnector before OpenConnector can return a pspPaymentId), so PaymentTransactionController does not currently persist it on any code path. Declared here for forward-compatibility; see PaymentTransactionController::initiate()'s docblock.
pspPaymentId.title: PSP Payment ID

amount.description: Amount of this payment attempt. PaymentTransactionController::initiate() always computes this server-side from the Order's remaining balance and never accepts a client-supplied amount. KNOWN GAP (documented, not fabricated): OpenRegister's generic object-create API carries no role restriction on this schema — an admin-only x-openregister-authorization.create (mirroring XapiStatement's stopgap) would also block initiate()'s own non-admin payer callers, and ObjectService::runAsSystem()'s own docblock warns against wrapping user-triggered writes in elevated system trust — so a payer who bypassed the controller and POSTed directly to the OR object API could still set an arbitrary amount. This residual risk is acknowledged here rather than silently assumed away; see PaymentTransactionController::initiate()'s docblock.
amount.title: Amount

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code. Defaults to EUR.
currency.title: Currency

initiatedBy.description: Nextcloud user ID of the caller who initiated this payment attempt.
initiatedBy.title: Initiated By

initiatedAt.description: When this payment attempt was initiated.
initiatedAt.title: Initiated At

completedAt.description: When this payment attempt reached a terminal state (succeeded/failed/expired/cancelled). Not populated by this change's callback() implementation — a documented follow-up, not a fabricated guarantee (see PaymentTransactionController docblock).
completedAt.title: Completed At

lifecycle.description: PaymentTransaction lifecycle state. Managed by the OR lifecycle engine; do not set directly.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

x-openregister-lifecycle._comment: initiate is driven by PaymentTransactionController::initiate() itself once OpenConnector's launch-initiation call succeeds (moving the freshly-created pending row to awaiting-redirect). fail additionally allows pending -> failed directly, for the case where the outbound OpenConnector call itself fails synchronously inside initiate() before any redirect ever happened. succeed/expire/cancel/refund (and the awaiting-redirect branch of fail) are driven by PaymentTransactionController::callback() (OpenConnector's PSP adapter reporting a status update) — never called directly by a raw OR object-API client.

x-property-rbac._comment: Mirrors FinalGrade's block, adapted to this schema's actual fields (PaymentTransaction has no payerId/learnerId of its own): the caller who initiated the payment (initiatedBy) reads their own PaymentTransaction; admin/finance roles read all.

Entitlement.title: Entitlement
Entitlement.description: What a paid, non-voluntary Order grants (e.g. access to a paid Course). Structurally cannot become a gate for a voluntary fee: the grant transition (pending -> active) requires BOTH FeeItemVoluntaryEntitlementGuard (refuses if the referenced FeeItem.voluntary is true, per the Wet vrijwillige ouderbijdrage) AND EntitlementOrderPaidGuard (refuses unless the referenced Order has reached paid). Nothing in this capability gates access on a pending Entitlement — only active ones grant anything — so it is structurally impossible for a voluntary fee to ever become an access gate through this capability's own mechanism. See openspec/changes/school-payments/.

feeItemId.description: UUID of the FeeItem this Entitlement is for. FeeItemVoluntaryEntitlementGuard reads this FeeItem's voluntary flag to decide whether the grant transition may ever succeed.
feeItemId.title: Fee Item ID

orderLineId.description: UUID of the OrderLine this Entitlement is for. EntitlementOrderPaidGuard resolves this OrderLine's parent Order to check whether payment has completed.
orderLineId.title: Order Line ID

learnerId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the learner this Entitlement is for.
learnerId.title: Learner ID

grantedResourceKind.description: What kind of resource this Entitlement grants access to once active.
grantedResourceKind.title: Granted Resource Kind

grantedResourceId.description: Identifies the specific resource granted (e.g. a Course id when grantedResourceKind is course-access). Deliberately not a $ref: the target schema is polymorphic, varying per grantedResourceKind (Course, Cohort, or others) — a single canonical relation cannot name one target schema, per ADR-062 rule 7's own single-target assumption.
grantedResourceId.title: Granted Resource ID

grantedAt.description: When the grant transition succeeded. Null until active.
grantedAt.title: Granted At

lifecycle.description: Entitlement lifecycle state. Managed by the OR lifecycle engine; do not set directly.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

x-openregister-lifecycle._comment: requires is a single DI-tag string in this register's lifecycle engine (verified: ReportPeriodLockGuard's own docblock/changelog entry — OpenRegister's LifecycleAnnotationValidator rejects a non-string requires value) — there is no 'second requires entry' array shape. FeeItemVoluntaryEntitlementGuard composes OCA\Scholiq\Lifecycle\EntitlementOrderPaidGuard internally (constructor-injected, called after the voluntary check passes), mirroring ReportPeriodLockGuard's own composition of FraudCaseBlockGuard. The voluntary check runs FIRST and short-circuits so a voluntary FeeItem is refused regardless of the linked Order's paid status, exactly as the spec's own scenario requires.

x-property-rbac._comment: Mirrors FinalGrade's block: the learner reads their own Entitlement; admin/finance roles read all. A learner MUST NOT read another learner's Entitlement.

AccessibilityStatement.title: AccessibilityStatement
AccessibilityStatement.description: The toegankelijkheidsverklaring (accessibility statement) record required by the Tijdelijk besluit digitale toegankelijkheid overheid (BDTO, BWBR0040936). Fields mirror the toegankelijkheidsverklaring.nl invulassistent's field order. Lifecycle: draft → published → archived; the publish transition requires AccessibilityStatementPublishGuard, which refuses to publish an unsubstantiated claim (missing evaluation evidence, or a fully-compliant status while a known limitation is still open). See openspec/changes/accessibility-conformance-statement/.

channelTitle.description: Human-readable name of the digital channel (app/site) this statement describes — e.g. the school's Scholiq environment name.
channelTitle.title: Channel Title

status.description: Conformance status, collapsed from the Dutch government model's five-level A–E scale (toegankelijkheidsverklaring.nl): A (voldoet volledig) maps to fully-compliant; B/C (voldoet gedeeltelijk, with or without an approved remediation plan) both map to partially-compliant — the B/C plan-approval distinction is a Logius-tracked procedural fact this schema does not itself carry; D (voldoet niet) maps to non-compliant; E (geen verklaring) is a non-state that cannot apply to a record that exists. Whoever transcribes this statement into the official invulassistent picks B or C using their own plan-approval status. Null until set; required before publish.
status.title: Conformance Status

evaluationMethod.description: How the conformance status above was determined. Null until set; required before publish.
evaluationMethod.title: Evaluation Method

evaluationDate.description: Date the evaluation method above was carried out. Null until set; required before publish.
evaluationDate.title: Evaluation Date

researchReportUrl.description: Optional link to the full research/audit report backing this statement. Null when no separate report exists.
researchReportUrl.title: Research Report URL

standardApplied.description: The accessibility standard this statement is assessed against.
standardApplied.title: Standard Applied

feedbackContact.description: Contact mechanism (email address or web form) a person can use to report an accessibility barrier or ask a question about this statement. Null until set; required before publish.
feedbackContact.title: Feedback Contact

escalationRoute.description: The school's own complaints procedure plus the statutory escalation to the Nationale Ombudsman if the school does not respond satisfactorily, per the BDTO.
escalationRoute.title: Escalation Route

lastReviewedAt.description: Date this statement was last reviewed/actualised. Drives the reviewOverdue calculation below — the BDTO requires the statement to be reviewed at least once a year.
lastReviewedAt.title: Last Reviewed At

approvedBy.description: Name of the official who approved/signed off this statement.
approvedBy.title: Approved By

approvedByRole.description: Role/title of the approving official (e.g. principal, bestuurder).
approvedByRole.title: Approved By Role

lifecycle.description: AccessibilityStatement lifecycle state. Managed by the OR lifecycle engine; do not set directly.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

x-openregister-authorization._comment: The statement is the school's official record — authoring/updating is restricted the same way BsaWarning/xapi-statement restrict theirs. Reporting a barrier (AccessibilityFeedback, below) is deliberately the opposite posture.

reviewOverdue.description: True when lastReviewedAt is set and more than 365 days in the past — the BDTO's 'at least once a year' actualisation duty. Pure JSON-logic dateDiff/lt idiom, reusing Enrolment.isOverdue's @now-comparison shape; no PHP TimedJob.

subject.nl: De toegankelijkheidsverklaring is aan een jaarlijkse herziening toe
subject.en: The accessibility statement is due for its annual review

AccessibilityLimitation.title: AccessibilityLimitation
AccessibilityLimitation.description: The known-limitations register — one row per known accessibility issue disclosed under an AccessibilityStatement, the honest evidence core of the accessibility-conformance capability. Lifecycle: open → mitigated → fixed. See openspec/changes/accessibility-conformance-statement/.

accessibilityStatementId.description: UUID of the AccessibilityStatement this limitation is disclosed under.
accessibilityStatementId.title: Accessibility Statement ID

wcagCriterion.description: The WCAG 2.1 success criterion this limitation relates to.
wcagCriterion.title: WCAG Criterion

affectedSurface.description: The page or component affected by this limitation.
affectedSurface.title: Affected Surface

description.description: Plain-language description of the limitation.
description.title: Description

justification.description: Why this limitation is not yet fixed, including whether it is claimed as a disproportionate-burden exception (see disproportionateBurden below).
justification.title: Justification

disproportionateBurden.description: Whether this limitation is claimed as a disproportionate-burden exception under the government model, rather than a planned fix.
disproportionateBurden.title: Disproportionate Burden

workaround.description: A workaround a user can use in the meantime, if any.
workaround.title: Workaround

plannedFixDate.description: Date the fix is planned for. Null only when genuinely undetermined.
plannedFixDate.title: Planned Fix Date

lifecycle.description: AccessibilityLimitation lifecycle state. Managed by the OR lifecycle engine; do not set directly.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State

x-openregister-authorization._comment: Same authoring posture as AccessibilityStatement — mirrors xapi-statement/BsaWarning's create restriction.

AccessibilityFeedback.title: AccessibilityFeedback
AccessibilityFeedback.description: A user-reported accessibility barrier — the BDTO's required feedback mechanism, reachable by any authenticated user (the opposite RBAC posture from AccessibilityStatement/AccessibilityLimitation, deliberately: restricting who may report a barrier would defeat the requirement's own purpose). Lifecycle: submitted → acknowledged → resolved. See openspec/changes/accessibility-conformance-statement/.

reporterUserId.description: Nextcloud user ID of the person reporting the barrier.
reporterUserId.title: Reporter User ID

affectedSurface.description: The page or component the barrier was encountered on.
affectedSurface.title: Affected Surface

description.description: Plain-language description of the barrier encountered.
description.title: Description

severity.description: Self-reported severity of the barrier, on the same scale as AccessibilityLimitation.severity.
severity.title: Severity

triagedIntoLimitationId.description: UUID of the AccessibilityLimitation this feedback was triaged into, set by a compliance officer. Null until triaged.
triagedIntoLimitationId.title: Triaged Into Limitation ID

lifecycle.description: AccessibilityFeedback lifecycle state. Managed by the OR lifecycle engine; do not set directly.
lifecycle.title: Lifecycle State
